title: CI Staff Record Study

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: CI Staff Record Study
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the 29 November 1976 memorandum to Chief Information Management Staff, "CI Staff Record Study" from David H. Blee (Chief, Counterintelligence Staft) as well as records set a, b, d, e, f, g, h, and i described on pages two and three of the memo.

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers in outlets such as MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, AND Magazine, Unicorn Riot, and more. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive and and the journalist non-profit, MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.

Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category.

As my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it and making the raw data available, I request that fees be waived.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The 2022 annual budget report for the Office of Public Affairs of the Federal Bureau of Investigation.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1674627-000
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to appeal the determination of FOIA request 1674627-000, in which I requested the budget of the FBI's Office of Public Affairs for 2022.

In your response, you sent me a link to a speech delivered in 2021 by the former FBI director before the Senate Appropriations Committee. While this speech does contain certain information about the FBI budget, it does not mention the Office of Public Affairs specifically and is therefore not responsive to my request.

I therefore believe that my request has not been fulfilled in accordance with FOIA. I reiterate my request for the 2022 annual budget report for the FBI's Office of Public Affairs.

Respectfully,

Brett Carollo

title: Anonymous + general hacktivists 2009-2018 (Defense Intelligence Agency)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Anonymous + general hacktivists 2009-2018 (Defense Intelligence Agency)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Documents mentioning or relating to Anonymous (the hacker movement/collective, see below) or hacktivism (defined as hacking as a form of protest and/or activism) generated between 1 January 2009 and 1 January 2019, including but not limited to:

* Internal reports, bulletins, warnings and alerts relating to Anonymous or hacktivism
* Reports from the appropriate IT (Information Technology) or computer offices regarding possible cyber attacks by Anonymous or other hacktivists
* Reports, bulletins, warnings and alerts relating to or mentioning Anonymous or hacktivism sent to or received from any federal law enforcement or intelligence agencies or any fusion center
* Incident reports investigating possible hacking activity falling within the office's jurisdiction and allegedly carried out by members of Anonymous or other hacktivist groups
* Materials generated as a result of any suspected or actual compromise, breach or "dox" (revealing of personal or private information) by Anonymous or other hacktivists

Anonymous has been described by government agencies as a non-hierarchical hacktivist collective, Anonymous uses hacking (and arguably cracking) techniques to register political protest in campaigns known as “#ops.” Best known for their distributed denial of services (DDoS) attacks, past activities have included attacks against the Church of Scientology; Visa, Paypal, and others who withdrew their services from WikiLeaks’ Julian Assange after that group began releasing war documents; #OpTunisia and others purporting to support the Arab Spring; and a campaign that brought down the website of the Westboro Baptist Church. #Ops are usually marked with the release of a video of a reader in a Guy Fawkes mask using a computer generated voice. See attached for additional background information.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best
An interim response, stating the request is being processed.
A copy of documents responsive to the request.

title: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request seeks all records from 1995 (or earlier) to 2010 (or later if available) referencing Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (also known as “KB Reid”), a U.S. Army Military Intelligence officer who was selected for the Military Intelligence Excepted Career Program (GREAT SKILL) in 1987 and subsequently served in some of the most sensitive Army and interagency HUMINT roles of the post–Cold War and post-9/11 era.

I respectfully request:

	1.	Personnel Records
	•	Official Military Personnel File (OMPF), Officer Evaluation Reports (OERs), commendations, promotions, awards, fitness reports, assignment orders, and internal performance evaluations for Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (SSN/Service Number can be provided if required).
	•	Decorations including: Defense Superior Service Medal, Bronze Star (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Defense Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Joint Service Commendation Medal, Army Commendation Medal (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Army Achievement Medal, Ranger tab, Special Forces tab, Pathfinder, Master Parachutist, and Military Free Fall Jump Master badges ￼.
	2.	Program and Command Assignments
	•	All records documenting his service in the GREAT SKILL Program, including appointment, reassignment memos, or internal assessments.
	•	His service as Regional Desk Officer and Special Assistant for Military Affairs at a national-level intelligence agency, with a focus on East Asian and Near East operations.
	•	Records from his time as Commander of a Defense HUMINT Service base in Bosnia, and as CJ2 for Combined Joint Task Force Fervent Archer.
	•	Documentation from his command of a sensitive HUMINT collection detachment deployed to Afghanistan (2002), where he served as the first HUMINT case officer supporting U.S. Special Operations Command (USSOCOM).
	•	Records from his second Afghanistan deployment (2004), where he commanded a HUMINT base supporting surge operations during Afghanistan’s first democratic elections.
	•	Internal records, rosters, orders, or awards tied to his 2005 Iraq deployment as a Senior HUMINT Advisor in a sensitive interagency coordination cell.
	3.	Strategic Instruction & Final Command
	•	Records of his time as Chief of the Military Group and Senior DoD Instructor at a joint interagency strategic intelligence training institution (likely DIA or a multi-agency DoD school).
	•	Materials from his command of the U.S. Army Field Support Center (AFSC) beginning in May 2009, including speeches, directives, or internal comms.
	•	Any documentation of his death from pancreatic cancer in 2010, honors received, or correspondence related to his funeral and burial at Arlington National Cemetery on September 9, 2010.
	4.	Legacy Sites and Honors
	•	Records regarding the 2011 naming of the DIA Contingency Operating Base in Kabul as Camp K.B. Reid, including naming justification, interagency approvals, and dedication ceremony photos or memos.
	•	Materials related to the Reid Center (DIA’s training facility in Norfolk, VA, established in 2012 and now closed).
	•	The establishment of the COL Kurush Bharucha-Reid Award for Excellence in HUMINT Tradecraft in 2014 by the HUMINT Training – Joint Center of Excellence, including award criteria, selection board minutes, and awardee names.
	•	Any correspondence, ceremony documentation, or Hall of Fame entry details from his 2014 induction into the Military Intelligence Corps Hall of Fame ￼.
	5.	Photographic and Ceremonial Records
	•	All official photographs, eulogies, obituaries, citations, press releases, internal honors, or other agency acknowledgments related to Col. Bharucha-Reid, both during service and posthumously.
	6.	Classified and Segregable Records
	•	If any records are classified, please initiate a mandatory declassification review (MDR) under Executive Order 13526 and release all segregable non-exempt portions under FOIA guidelines.
	7.	Delivery and Fee Waiver
	•	Please provide documents in searchable PDF format via secure email or download link.

This request supports a public-interest historical and educational project—specifically, a feature film titled Undercover Patriot exploring the intersection of identity, secrecy, and service. 

As a fellow Parsi Zoroastrian American, I believe Col. Bharucha-Reid’s legacy deserves recognition beyond the classified corridors in which he served.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adil Minocherhomjee

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Lebanon School District/SAU88)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Lebanon School District/SAU88)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: Navy support for "Flight Ready" (2024), short film for Apple Vision Pro

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Navy support for "Flight Ready" (2024), short film for Apple Vision Pro
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Navy and the production of the 2024 short film "Flight Ready," produced for the Apple Vision Pro. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Navy and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Flight Ready," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Flight Ready" appeared (under the title "Flightdeck") in a list for Navy-supported productions for the year 2024 that was released to me through FOIA. That list is attached to this request. 

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

"Top Guns: The Next Generation" (as "Nat Geo Docu-Series on Student Naval Aviators") appears in a list of Navy-supported productions for the year 2024 that was released to me through FOIA. That list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. title: Public Records Request: Flock audits (Police Department - Beverly, MA)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits (Police Department - Beverly, MA)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: ONDCP Director Gil Kerlikowske's Travel Records for March 1  - May 10, 2011

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: ONDCP Director Gil Kerlikowske's Travel Records for March 1  - May 10, 2011
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any records -- itineraries, daily schedules, press releases and other documents -- reflecting former Office of National Drug Control Policy Director Gil Kerlikowske's official travel and public appearances between March 1, 2011 and May 10, 2011. (When I made this request to ONDCP, the agency said it did not have any of the records I was seeking, and, because of the time period in question -- 2011 -- suggested I send the request to NARA, instead.)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Philip Eil

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request: ONDCP Director Gil Kerlikowske's Travel Records for March 1 - May 10, 2011
communications.communication:
This is an automated reply to inform you that the National Archives and Records Administration received your email.  

We have received your request and will respond within 20 working days. Please note that submitting duplicate requests will increase wait times.  

If you have any questions, you can call our office at 301-837-3190. You can also visit our web page at www.archives.gov/research/foia.  

Notice to NARA Researchers and FOIA Requesters

Due to the COVID-19 pandemic and pursuant to guidance received from the Office of Management and Budget (OMB), NARA has adjusted its normal operations to balance the need of completing its mission-critical work while also adhering to the recommended social distancing for the safety of our staff. As a result of this re-prioritization of activities, you may experience a delay in receiving an initial acknowledgment as well as a substantive response to your reference or FOIA request or appeal.  We apologize for this inconvenience and appreciate your understanding and patience.


Thank you.

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request: ONDCP Director Gil Kerlikowske's Travel Records for March 1 - May 10, 2011
communications.communication:
Dear Philip Eil:

I am writing in response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request
received by this office on April 15, 2022 for records in the custody of the
National Archives and Records Administration (NARA). Your request was
assigned FOIA Tracking Number *RD 67366*.

We are continuing to work on logging in FOIA requests in the order they are
received. Once your request reaches the front of the initial response
queue, a staff member will search for the record(s) and provide you with a
more substantive response detailing the results of the search and the
estimated completion date for your request.

Our small staff will continue to work diligently to process each request as
quickly as possible, but please be aware that we are operating at
significantly reduced staffing levels onsite and it is taking longer than
usual. We apologize for this inconvenience and appreciate your
understanding and patience.

If you have any questions concerning this action, or to notify this office
of a change in your contact information, please email
specialaccess_foia@nara.gov or call 301-837-3190. All communications
concerning this request should reference your FOIA Tracking Number *RD
67366*.

Sincerely,

Ashney Randle
Government Information Specialist
Special Access & FOIA Program (RF)
National Archives at College Park

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request #RD 67366
communications.communication:
Dear Philip Eil:

Unfortunately, the COVID-19 pandemic has created many unprecedented
challenges in our work. The majority of the records that our customers
request require our staff to be on-site to search for and review microfilm,
microfiche, or original textual records. Even as we expand our reopening,
operations may be temporarily curtailed due to limited staffing and/or
deteriorating local health conditions.

Due to limitations on staff in our facility over the course of the
pandemic, the backlog of FOIA requests has significantly expanded, and we
are continuing to work on logging in and providing initial responses to the
FOIA requests we received over the past 26 months.

When we receive a request, we inform you in writing that we received your
request and provide you with a FOIA tracking number. Then, your request is
placed in our initial response queue waiting for the next available staff
member to conduct a search. Once the search is complete, you will be
contacted directly and informed of the results of the search and provided
an estimated completion date for the line-by-line review of the file as
requested under FOIA. Our small staff has and will continue to work
diligently to process each request as quickly as possible.

I want to assure you that we are aware of your request, and at this time,
your request is still in our initial search queue waiting for a search to
be conducted. Due to the size of the backlog at this time, we are not able
to provide you with an estimated timeframe of when someone will reach your
request and start the search process.

We appreciate your continued patience.

Sincerely,

Ashney Randle
Government Information Specialist
Special Access & FOIA Program (RF)
National Archives at College Park

I want to assure you that we are aware of your request, and at this time,
your request is still in our initial search queue waiting for a search to
be conducted. Due to the size of the backlog at this time, we are not able
to provide you with an estimated timeframe of when someone will reach your
request and start the search process.

*Please note, we will not respond to requests for status updates more than
once every six months. *

We appreciate your continued patience.

Sincerely,

Ashney Randle
Government Information Specialist
Special Access & FOIA Program (RF)
National Archives at College Park

communications.subject: Re: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #RD 67366
communications.communication:
This is an automated reply to inform you that the National Archives and Records Administration received your email.  

We have received your request and will respond within 20 working days. Please note that submitting duplicate requests will increase wait times.  

If you have any questions, you can call our office at 301-837-3190. You can also visit our web page at www.archives.gov/research/foia.  

Notice to NARA Researchers and FOIA Requesters

Due to the COVID-19 pandemic and pursuant to guidance received from the Office of Management and Budget (OMB), NARA has adjusted its normal operations to balance the need of completing its mission-critical work while also adhering to the recommended social distancing for the safety of our staff. As a result of this re-prioritization of activities, you may experience a delay in receiving an initial acknowledgment as well as a substantive response to your reference or FOIA request or appeal.  We apologize for this inconvenience and appreciate your understanding and patience.


Thank you.

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request #RD 67366
communications.communication:
Dear Philip Eil:

This is in response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated
April 15, 2022 for records in the custody of the National Archives and
Records Administration (NARA). You requested access to “any records --
itineraries, daily schedules, press releases and other documents --
reflecting former Office of National Drug Control Policy Director Gil
Kerlikowske's official travel and public appearances between March 1, 2011
and May 10, 2011.” Your request was received in this office on April 20,
2022 and assigned reference number RD 67366.

We have taken legal custody of the emails of the Federal components of the
Executive Office of the President from the Obama administration and were
able to search them for records that could be responsive to your request.

We have attached a spreadsheet that details the different search parameters
we used as well as the resulting number of potentially responsive documents
for each search.

Column A provides the basic search parameters that were used. We searched
for 1.) emails sent from Kerlikowske 2.) emails sent to Kerlikowske 3.)
emails Kerlikowske was CC’d on 4.) emails Kerlikowske was BCC’d on.

Column B provides the number of search results using only the basic search
parameters from Column A.This column provides the overall number of emails
sent to/from Kerlikowske regarding any/all topics during the requested date
range. For example, block B2 indicates that Kerikowske sent 6,081 total
emails during the requested time frame.

Column C adds a filter to the search results of Column B. It filters out
some spam mail. The emails excluded include the following words: "newsletter"
"e-newsletter" "newspaper article"  "daily article" "news summary" "public
mail" "public opinion mail"  "clip" "clips"

Column D provides the number of search results using key words mentioned in
your request. The key words used were: "itinerary" "itineraries" "schedule"
"press release" "press releases" "travel" "appearance" "appearances" "event"

Column E adds a filter to the search results of Column D. It filters out
some spam mail. The emails excluded include the following words: "newsletter"
"e-newsletter" "newspaper article"  "daily article" "news summary" "public
mail" "public opinion mail"  "clip" "clips"

Please let us know which set of searches you would like us to consider
responsive to your search. While making your decision, please keep in mind
the amount of time it will take to process requests of varying sizes. If
your total request is for less than 141 responsive emails, it can be added
to our first tier processing queue, which has an estimated processing time
of 39 months. Requests for 141 - 600 emails will be added to our second
tier processing queue.  Reviewers are currently processing second tier
requests that were received in June 2014.  Requests for more than 600
emails will be added to our third tier processing queue and processed on an
incremental basis.

Are you interested in receiving correspondence regarding automatic replies
to emails, out of office responses, notifications of canceled meetings, and
notifications of accepting or declining invitations? These types of
materials are currently included in the search results. Please let us know
if you would like us to exclude these types of materials. This option might
help in narrowing the results. Additionally, if there are any other
keywords you are particularly interested in which might narrow the results
more, we would be happy to run another set of searches.

These records require screening for categories of information exempted from
disclosure under the terms of the FOIA prior to public release. In
particular, information may be redacted to protect the personal privacy of
living individuals.

Given the initial search results, your request has been assigned to our
third tier processing queue. This is a temporary assignment until we know
your preferred search parameters and the number of records we will need to
review. Please contact me directly to let me know how you would like us to
proceed.

You must notify this office of changes to your contact information. If
communication is returned as undeliverable and no return address is
provided, your case will be administratively closed. To change your contact
information or to track the status of your request, please email
specialaccess_foia@nara.gov or call 301-837-3190. Please include your FOIA
tracking number in all communications concerning this request.

If you are not satisfied with our action on this request and would like the
opportunity to discuss our response, you may contact our FOIA Public
Liaison for assistance:

Accessioned Executive Branch Records – Washington, DC Area

FOIA Requester Service Center: 301-837-3190

FOIA Public Liaison: Rebecca Calcagno

8601 Adelphi Road, Room 5500

College Park, MD 20740-6001

dc.foia.liaison@nara.gov

301-837-3190


If you have other questions regarding your request, please contact me
directly.



Sincerely,


Brigitte Flynn

Archives Specialist

Special Access and FOIA Program (RF)
National Archives at College Park, MD

https://www.archives.gov/research/foia

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #RD 67366
communications.communication:
Hi, Brigitte 

Thank you for your email, and my apologies for the delayed response.

Given the scale of the records I initially asked about, I'm wondering if it would be possible to narrow the window of my inquiry to between April 17th and April 22nd, 2011.

Thus, my new request would be:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any records -- itineraries, daily schedules, press releases and other documents -- reflecting former Office of National Drug Control Policy Director Gil Kerlikowske's official travel and public appearances between April 17, 2011 and April 22, 2011.  

Please advise me if you would need any additional info from me before proceeding with the fulfillment of this updated request.

Thank you!

-Phil 

philipeil.com 
401 374 5868 
philip.edward.eil@gmail.com
@phileil 
facebook.com/phileilwriter

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request #RD 67366
communications.communication:
Requester Philip Eil--


This is in response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request
dated April 15, 2022 for records in the custody of the National Archives
and Records Administration (NARA). You initially requested access to “any
records -- itineraries, daily schedules, press releases and other documents
-- reflecting former Office of National Drug Control Policy Director Gil
Kerlikowske's official travel and public appearances between March 1,
2011 and May 10, 2011.” Your request was received in this office on April
20, 2022 and assigned reference number RD 67366.


On November 29, 2022, you requested to narrow the window of your request to
between April 17 and April 22, 2011.  This narrows the results to a total
of 37 emails.  I have attached a worksheet that details the search terms I
used to determine this number. Please note that there may be overlap
between the various search results lists.


These records require review for information protected from release under
the FOIA: https://www.archives.gov/foia/foia-guide#determine.

As narrowed, I can move your request from the third tier processing queue
to our faster first-tier processing queue. Taking into consideration our
existing backlog, we estimate it will take 39 months to complete processing.

Once we notify you that processing is complete, you may either visit our
College Park, MD facility to view the file at no cost, or order a
reproduction for a fee according to our fee schedule
<https://www.archives.gov/research/order/fees>.

You must notify this office of changes to your contact information. If
communication is returned as undeliverable and no return address is
provided, your case will be administratively closed. To change your contact
information or to track the status of your request, please email
specialaccess_foia@nara.gov or call 301-837-3190. Please include your FOIA
tracking number in all communications concerning this request.

If you are not satisfied with our action on this request and would like the
opportunity to discuss our response, you may contact our FOIA Public
Liaison for assistance:

Accessioned Executive Branch Records – Washington, DC Area

FOIA Requester Service Center: 301-837-3190

FOIA Public Liaison: Rebecca Calcagno

8601 Adelphi Road, Room 5500

College Park, MD 20740-6001

dc.foia.liaison@nara.gov

301-837-3190

If you have other questions regarding your request, please contact me
directly. Ms. Flynn, with whom you have had prior contact on this request,
has transferred to another division.


This email is UNCLASSIFIED.


Dr. A. M. Weimer

Supervisory Archivist

Special Access and FOIA Program (RF)

(pronouns: they/them, she/her)

National Archives and Records Administration

8601 Adelphi Road, Room 5510

College Park, MD 20740-6001

Amanda.Weimer@nara.gov

(301) 837-0604

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request #RD 67366
communications.communication:
Dear  Philip Eil,

You requested a status update on *RD 67366*, a request our office received 
on April 15, 2022 and to which we initially replied on September 9, 2022. 
The request was assigned to our Tier 1 processing queue for review.

To give you an indication of our existing backlog for cases in Tier 1, 
there are approximately 880 cases ahead of yours. Unfortunately, the 
COVID-19 pandemic has created many unprecedented challenges in our work. 
The majority of the records that our customers request require our staff to 
be on-site to search for and review microfilm, microfiche, or original 
textual records. Staffing levels on-site have been severely limited with 
long periods where our facility was closed. Even as we expand our 
reopening, operations may be temporarily curtailed due to limited staffing 
and/or deteriorating local health conditions.

*Please note, we will not respond to status update requests more than once 
every six months.*

To notify this office of a change in your contact information or to track 
the status of your request, please telephone 301-837-3190 or e-mail 
specialaccess_foia@nara.gov. All communications concerning this request 
should reference your case tracking number *RD 67366*.

Sincerely,

Jason Mincey
Archives Specialist
Special Access & FOIA Program (RF)

communications.subject: National Archives FOIA request RD 67366
communications.communication:
Dear Philip Eil:

I completed processing the records you requested from the National Archives
and Records Administration under Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) tracking
number RD 67366.

You requested access to emails of Office of National Drug Control Policy
Director Gil Kerlikowske dated 3/1/2011-5/10/2011.

We conducted searches in our Electronic Records Archives (ERA) for this
subject which resulted in 4 search results lists (SRL) with responsive
records.


I completed a line-by-line review of the records you requested. Please be
advised, I have considered the foreseeable harm standard when reviewing
records and applying FOIA exemptions for this request. Information was
released to the greatest extent possible.

Below is a summary of my review:

Responsive Search Results Lists (SRL)

Total Emails

Responsive Emails

Non-Responsive Emails*

Emails Released in Full

Emails Released in Part

Emails Withheld in Full

BCC Kerlikowske SRL

9

9

0

5

4
0

CC Kerlikowske SRL

2

2

0

2

0
0

From Kerlikowske SRL

9

9

0

6

3
0

To Kerlikowske SRL

17

17

0

15

2
0

Total

37

37

0

28

9
*0*



I withheld information protected under section (b) of the FOIA (5 U.S.C.
552). The specific reasons for the denials are:

TYPE OF INFORMATION WITHHELD

FOIA EXEMPTION or STATUTE

privileged or confidential trade secrets and commercial or financial
information

(b)(4)

personal privacy of living individuals

(b)(6)

You or your designated representative may view the files at the National
Archives at College Park, Maryland. We require at least one week advance
notice to prepare for your visit and ask that you email
specialaccess_foia@nara.gov with your FOIA tracking number (RD 67366) and
the date you plan to visit. Please also let us know if you would like us to
book an appointment on your behalf. Please monitor our web page
https://www.archives.gov/college-park for details and updates, as the
operating status for the facility could change, affecting opening hours for
the research rooms.


If you are unable to visit and you wish to hire a researcher to duplicate
the files, information on how to hire a researcher can be found at this
link: https://www.archives.gov/research/hire-help. The National Archives
does not endorse anyone who appears on the list.  It is possible the fees
to hire a researcher may be less than the reproduction fees.

If you cannot visit our College Park facility, you may order a reproduction
copy of the files at $20 per one .zip file (i.e. per SRL), or $17 for 2-10
files. To place a reproduction order, please contact our office at
specialaccess_foia@nara.gov or 301-837-3190. A mailing/billing address is
required to generate a price quote, so please include that information with
your reproduction request.


If you are not satisfied with my action on this request, you have the right
to file an administrative appeal within ninety (90) calendar days from the
date of this letter. Appeals must be in writing and may be delivered by
regular U.S. mail or by email. By filing an appeal, you preserve your
rights under the Freedom of Information Act and present the deciding agency
with an opportunity to review your request and reconsider its decision. If
you submit your appeal by regular mail, it should be addressed to the
Deputy Archivist of the United States (ND), National Archives and Records
Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road, Room 4200, College Park, Maryland
20740-6001. Both the letter and envelope should be clearly marked “FOIA
Appeal.” If you submit your appeal by email, please send it to foia@nara.gov,
addressed to the Deputy Archivist, with the words “FOIA Appeal” in the
subject line. If you exercise your right to appeal, please explain why you
believe this response does not meet the requirements of the Freedom of
Information Act. All communications concerning this request should
reference your FOIA tracking number.

If you would like the opportunity to discuss my response and attempt to
resolve your dispute without initiating the appeals process, you may
contact our FOIA Public Liaison for assistance:

Accessioned Executive Branch Records – Washington, DC Area

FOIA Requester Service Center: 301-837-3190

FOIA Public Liaison: Rebecca Calcagno

8601 Adelphi Road, Room 5500

College Park, MD 20740-6001

dc.foia.liaison@nara.gov

301-837-3190

You may also contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS),
the Federal FOIA Ombudsman’s office, for assistance. OGIS offers mediation
services to help resolve disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal
agencies. The contact information for OGIS is:

Office of Government Information Services

National Archives and Records Administration

8601 Adelphi Road–OGIS

College Park, MD 20740-6001

Email: ogis@nara.gov

https://www.archives.gov/ogis

Phone: 202-741-5770 or 1-877-684-6448

This completes our response to your request. Your case is now closed.

Sincerely,

Robert Nowatzki


https://www.archives.gov/research/foia
-- 
Robert Nowatzki
Archivist
FOIA Program Area
Research Services, Transparency and Access Program (RPT)
National Archives and Records Administration
8601 Adelphi Road, Room 5510
College Park, MD 20740-6001
robert.nowatzki1@nara.gov
301-837-3683

title: full list of Doge Contracts (Department of Interior, Office of the Secretary)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: full list of Doge Contracts (Department of Interior, Office of the Secretary)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552), I hereby request access to the following records:

All contracts—whether interagency, vendor, or service agreements—that have been cancelled, terminated, or voided by your agency from January 1, 2019, to the present. This request includes but is not limited to:

Contract numbers, titles, and associated vendors or agencies.

Dates of contract initiation and cancellation.

The stated reasons for cancellation or termination.

Any notices of termination, memos, emails, or official correspondence regarding the cancellation.

Any reports, audits, or evaluations that contributed to or followed the contract's termination.

Any financial documentation reflecting penalties, refunds, or recoupments associated with the cancelled contracts.

If there are documents that are partially exempt, I request that all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions be released. The website doge.gov only releases about 30% of the records. 

This request is made in the public interest, and the records will be used to inform the public about government operations. Therefore, I respectfully request a waiver of all fees. However, if fees do apply, please inform me of any costs exceeding $50 before proceeding.  

I prefer to receive the requested records in electronic format, via email attachment if possible.

Thank you for your time and attention to this matter. I look forward to a response within the 20 business days required by statute.

Sungho Park

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sungho Park

title: Rocky Mountain HIDTA records (Division of Criminal Investigation)

communications.subject: Wyoming Public Records Act Request: Rocky Mountain HIDTA records (Division of Criminal Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wyoming Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All meeting minutes and agendas for the 2023-2026 for Rocky Mountain High Intensity Drug Trafficking Area (HIDTA) Executive Board. You agency has a seat on this board. (For reference: https://www.rmhidta.org/executive-board) 
2. The governing agreements for your agency's participation in the Rocky Mountain HIDTA program

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dave Maass
Director of Investigations
Electronic Frontier Foundation

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Re: Wyoming Public Records Act Request: Rocky Mountain HIDTA records (Division of Criminal Investigation)
communications.communication:
Hi Dave,

Attached are the award agreements for our HIDTA award for the years 23, 24
and 25.  This is the only formal agreement we have with RMHIDTA.  We are
not the custodian of the meeting minutes and agendas.  That request would
need to be made to RMHIDTA.

Thanks,
Megan

2424 Pioneer Avenue, 3rd Floor
Cheyenne, WY 82002
(307) 777-6710

of public 
business, is subject to the Wyoming Public Records

disclosed to third parties.

title: Scanner Contraband Reports

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Scanner Contraband Reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All "monthly reports of any contraband recovered as a result of body scanners" submitted by Facility Radiation Safety Officers to the Department Radiation Safety Officer in September, October, and November 2025, as required by DOCCS directive 4923 ("Use of Body Scanners").

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Chris Gelardi

communications.subject: Response - 26-FOI-00344
communications.communication:
May 4, 2026


Ben Heckscher

N/A

MuckRock News, DEPT MR205913, 263 Huntington Ave

Boston, MA  02115


Dear Heckscher:


This letter acknowledges the receipt of your FOIA request dated March 2, 2026 requesting To Whom It May Concern: Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records: The minutes for any meeting of the Amtrak Board of Directors that occurred during the month of December 2025. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires. Sincerely, Ben Heckscher Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2228277/.


This request has been issued tracking number 26-FOI-00344.  Please reference this number in all correspondence and communications with this office.


FOIA Exemption 5 (deliberative process privilege) applies to inter-agency or intra-agency records where, for instance, there is a work product that is in draft format or documents that contain deliberations between parties leading up to a decision.



Exemption 6 protects the privacy interest of individuals, whose substantial interest in personal identity protection outweighs any public interest link in disclosure of information that could be used to identify them.? In cases such as this, the disclosure of the enclosed records “would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy.”? See e.g., Pierce v. U.S. Air Force, 512 F.3d 184, 191 (5th Cir. 2007) (“To qualify as a similar file under Exemption 6…the information need only ‘appl[y] to the individual.”)??


Pursuant to Amtrak’s FOIA regulations (49 CFR 701.10), if you do not agree with Amtrak’s decision, you may file an appeal with William Herrmann, Executive Vice President, Chief Legal and Human Resources Officer, within ninety days (90) of the date of this letter, specifying the relevant facts and the basis for your appeal.? Your appeal may be mailed to Mr. Herrmann’s attention:? National Railroad Passenger Corporation, Law Department, One Massachusetts Avenue, NW, Washington, DC? 20001.? The President and CEO of Amtrak has delegated authority to the General Counsel and Corporate Secretary for the rules and compliance to the FOIA.? ?

If you have any questions regarding the processing of your request, please feel free to contact me at Rebecca.Conner@amtrak.com or at 202-906-3215.  For further assistance and to discuss any aspect of your request you may also contact our FOIA Public Liaison at FoiaPublicLiaison@amtrak.com.  Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS), National Archives and Records Administration, Room 2510, 8601 Adelphi Road, College Park, MD  20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov, telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or fax 202-741-5769.


Thank you,

FOIA Officer

title: OGC Legal Assessments regarding OBBBA Section 10107 and Byrd Rule Compliance

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: OGC Legal Assessments regarding OBBBA Section 10107 and Byrd Rule Compliance
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request all agency records from January 1, 2025, to September 1, 2025, including but not limited to formal legal opinions, final memoranda, and correspondence with the Senate Budget Committee, regarding the "Byrd Rule" compliance or "budgetary germaneness" of Section 10107 of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025 (OBBBA).
Please specifically include:
1. Final legal determinations regarding the USDA’s authority to implement the termination of SNAP-Ed funding via the reconciliation process.
2. Communications sent to or received from the Senate Parliamentarian or Senate Budget Committee staff regarding the "Byrd Bath" rulings issued on or about June 20, 2025.
3. Any Vaughn Index or detailed list of responsive documents withheld under Exemption 5, to ensure transparency regarding the existence of legal disputes over this provision.
I request that a search be conducted at minimum within theInternational Affairs, Food Assistance, and Farm and Rural Programs Division of the OGC, including the records of  Associate General Counsel Gena Kochan and her staff. However, this request is not  limited to this division; please search all offices within the OGC likely to possess  responsive records, including the Office of the General Counsel's immediate front office and the Office of Information Affairs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Lehman

communications.subject: FOIA Acknowledgment 2026-OGC-02919-F
communications.communication:
Dear Emma Lehman,

This email acknowledges receipt of your attached Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), Office of Information Affairs, Freedom of Information Act Division (OIA-FOIA).

Your request was received by this office February 12, 2026.

The OIA-FOIA is under the purview of the Office of the General Counsel (OGC) and serves as the focal point for USDA's FOIA program. It provides coordination and ensures agency-wide compliance with the FOIA. Additionally, the OIA-FOIA processes requests and appeals on behalf of the Office of the Secretary (OSEC), the Under Secretaries, USDA's staff offices, and the Research, Education and Economics and Trade and Foreign Agricultural Affairs mission areas.

The FOIA, 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(A)(i), requires USDA to make a determination in response to a FOIA request within 20 business days from its date of receipt. In unusual circumstances, the Federal agency time limit may be extended by 10 business days as stated in 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B). If your request seeks numerous records that will necessitate a wide-ranging search, including searches with components and possible consultations with components that have a substantial interest in the determination of this request, USDA will invoke a 10 business day extension for determination of your request. You have the option to narrow the scope of your request to limit the number of potentially responsive records.

Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request. Pursuant to USDA FOIA Regulations, Title 7 CFR §1.6(a), the USDA processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt.

Provisions of the FOIA allow USDA to recover part of the cost of complying with your request. For purposes of fee assessment, you have been categorized as a "news media" requester, pursuant to 7 CFR Appendix A to Subpart A of Part 1 Section 2(b)(4)(i). For your review and information, a copy of the USDA FOIA Fee Schedule is attached. In accordance with 7 CFR Appendix A to Subpart A of Part 1, Section 3(b), your fees shall be waived where the amount of the fee is $25.00 or less. As a news media requester the only fees that would apply to you are duplication costs beyond the first 100 pages. Because your response will be delivered electronically, duplication costs are not anticipated.

Due to the increasing number of FOIA requests received by this office, we may encounter some delay in processing your request.  Pursuant to USDA FOIA Regulations, Title 7 CFR §1.6(a), the USDA processes FOIA requests according to their order of receipt.  A search will be conducted for records responsive to your request. If any responsive records are located, they will be reviewed for determination of releasability. Please be assured that a FOIA analyst will respond to your request as expeditiously as possible. We appreciate your patience as we proceed with your request.

Your request has been assigned FOIA Case No. 2026-OGC-02919-F. Please refer to this case number in any future correspondence. If you have any questions regarding the processing of this request, please contact Roman Vayner by email at roman.vayner@usda.gov<mailto:roman.vayner@usda.gov> or USDAFOIA@usda.gov<mailto:USDAFOIA@usda.gov>. For additional information regarding USDA FOIA regulations and process, please refer to the information available on-line at Freedom of Information Act Division | USDA.<https://www.usda.gov/ogc/office-information-affairs/foia-division>

We appreciate the opportunity to assist you.

The OIA-FOIA Team


Roman Vayner
FOIA Analyst (Contractor)
Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) Division
Office of Information Affairs (OIA)

[cid:image001.png@01DCA059.B98580E0]
Office of the General Counsel (OGC)
1400 Independence Avenue, S.W., Washington, D.C., 20250






This electronic message contains information generated by the USDA solely for the intended recipients. Any unauthorized interception of this message or the use or disclosure of the information it contains may violate the law and subject the violator to civil or criminal penalties. If you believe you have received this message in error, please notify the sender and delete the email immediately.

communications.communication:
Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on "Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request" on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@ic.fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI's FOIPA Program is searching the FBI's indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 134 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 523 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,335 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 1,875 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
Record/Information Dissemination Section (RIDS) FBI-Information Management Division
170 Marcel Drive, Winchester, VA 22602-4843
Direct: (540) 868-4593
Fax: (540) 868-4391
Questions E-mail: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Gogebic County)
communications.communication:
Dear Gogebic County Clerk FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Gogebic County Clerk county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

title: Records Request

communications.subject: Delaware Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Delaware Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFP# 2-25-26

Title/Description: Stop Arm Surveillance / Camera System for Bus Fleet 

Issuing Entity: Colonial and Red Clay Consolidated School District

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 

All addenda to the solicitation, and any questions and answers (Q&A) or clarifications issued to vendors. 

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program. 

If any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please provide the non-exempt portions and cite the specific statutory basis for each withholding or redaction. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your acknowledgment. 

 Regards,

Jon Smith

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg, a citizen of Delaware, in coordination with Jon Smith.

communications.communication:
Good morning Anthony,

Would you be able to help us find Lake's county-wide assessment property tax data for a FOIA request?

I had requested this via a FOIA but was told the records do not exist... But then I found from MCL 211.34d(3) which seems to say that equalization puts this information together each year (it calls it a computation equalization delivers to the treasurer), so that's why I figured I would reach out to you to weigh in.

Thank you!
Alexander Munro
50 N Huron St, Unit B
Ypsilanti, MI 48197
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: FW: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Lake County)
communications.communication:
Hi Alexander,

Attached is the requested form that is required under MCL 211.34d(3).  Please note, this form only shows total taxable values for the various taxing authorities throughout the County and does not have individual property assessments on it as your FOIA request appears to be requesting.

I have also attached a copy of our equalization report.  This report goes into greater detail of the individual townships and cities within the County so it may be helpful if you are trying to compile assessment data for the County.

[cid:image001.png@01DCDBBC.EA386010]
Anthony E. Meyaard MMAO CES

800 Tenth Street
Baldwin MI 49304

title: Open Records Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Open Records Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

e

title: Emails and Map records relating to Flock ALRPs in Redmond

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Emails and Map records relating to Flock ALRPs in Redmond
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. A map, or equivalent document, that shows the current, planned, or installed locations of all Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) cameras within the city limits of Redmond. Specifically, I would like clarification on the status of the camera that was planned for NE 51st St @ 520 (WB). If a single map document does not exist, I request a list of the street addresses, cross-streets, or GPS coordinates for each camera. 

2. All email communications, including any attachments, sent to or received by any Redmond Police Department staff member with any email address ending in “@flocksafety.com” or any other known flock email domains. The requested date range for this correspondence is from January 1st 2024 to the date this request is processed. Ideally, this data is presented in a PST or other machine-readable format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey JJ Jr S.

communications.subject: Police Record Request :: W036425-032326
communications.communication:
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of 3/23/2026, Reference # W036425-032326 Dear Joey  JJ, The Redmond Police Records Division received your public records request on 3/23/2026: “To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. A map, or equivalent document, that shows the current, planned, or installed locations of all Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) cameras within the city limits of Redmond. Specifically, I would like clarification on the status of the camera that was planned for NE 51st St @ 520 (WB). If a single map document does not exist, I request a list of the street addresses, cross-streets, or GPS coordinates for each camera. 

2. All email communications, including any attachments, sent to or received by any Redmond Police Department staff member with any email address ending in “@flocksafety.com” or any other known flock email domains. The requested date range for this correspondence is from January 1st 2024 to the date this request is processed. Ideally, this data is presented in a PST or other machine-readable format. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Joey JJ Jr S. 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2248376/” Your request will be forwarded to the applicable staff for response.  Please be advised, if you have multiple requests currently active with our agency, we will work on them in a separate queue (in most cases, completing the first request received before starting the next request). If you are requesting a collision report, please be advised that collision reports for the State of Washington are also available through the Washington State Patrol website. You will be notified regarding the status of your request by 3/31/2026 (5 business days per RCW 42.56). 
 Cordially, 
 
 Police Public Disclosure Desk Redmond Police Department Police Records Portal 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://redmondwa.govqa.us/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=36425

communications.subject: Police Record Request :: W036425-032326
communications.communication:
--- City of Redmond --- 
 RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of 3/23/2026, Reference # W036425-032326 Dear Joey  JJ, The Redmond Police Records Division received your message: " Hello, The original request stated "Specifically, I would like clarification on the status of the camera that was planned for NE 51st St @ 520 (WB)." The excel file lists a camera at this location, but there is not one there. This seems like incorrect information. Please clarify. Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2279519/" All flock cameras were either deactivated or removed in November 2025, when Redmond city counsel voted to deactivate our flock camera program. There is no record of a status update as the program is paused indefinitely at this time. The list is of all locations where cameras were originally installed in June/July of 2025, all of which were deactivated in November of 2025. 
This request is now closed. If you have any follow-up questions regarding the closing of this request, please promptly contact us by responding to this message.  If you would like a review of your request, the process can be found in Section 10 of Redmond’s Public Records Disclosure Rules of Procedure. If you are seeking further records not covered in this request, please submit a new request through our portal.  This request is closed and the City is no longer fulfilling it. Per state law (RCW 42.56.550), the Public Records Act’s one-year statute of limitations starts on this date. The statute of limitations defines the maximum time limit to file a lawsuit if you determine your records request was not fulfilled appropriately. 
Cordially, 
Police Public Disclosure Desk Redmond Police Department Police Records Portal

title: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in San Bernardino County in that year. Alameda County has an example of these records posted for the 2024 General Election on their Election Information for November 2024 under the name “Cast Vote Records (CVR) Report”.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

communications.subject: [External Message Added] San Bernardino County public records request #25-30627
communications.communication:
San Bernardino County ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-30627: Dear Aleksandra, A Cast Vote Record Report that contains ballot data only and is void of any voter information may be extracted and compiled. In order to provide you with the Cast Vote Record Reports (not ballot images) the County will need to extract and compile the data. It is estimated it will take ROV staff approximately one hour and fifteen minutes to generate the data for the November 2022 election and approximately one hour and fifteen minutes to generate the data for the November 2024 election. The fee for this process is $310.13 per hour. The total amount estimated to fulfill your request is $620.26. The records for the report you have requested for the November 2022 election are currently set for destruction in accordance with Elections Code section 17300 et seq. in approximately 10 days. In order to provide you with the Cast Vote Record Report for the November 2022 election please submit the fee of $310.13 within the next 10 days to the San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters at 777 E. Rialto Ave., San Bernardino, CA 92415. Once the Registrar of Voters has received your payment for either the November 2022 election, November 2024 election or both, the County will begin to extract and compile the information requested. If payment is not received within the allotted 10 days, the Cast Vote Record Report for the November 2022 election will be destroyed as scheduled. Sincerely, Thalia Amador Elections Specialist ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at San Bernardino County.

Technical support: See our help page
