communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request #25-30640
communications.communication:
Hi there,

Thank you for the timely and helpful information, and for giving me advanced notice regarding the timeline for the destruction of the November 2022 election records. I unfortunately was not able to get back to you in a timely manner, and so I understand the records are now permanently out of reach. Thank you for alerting me in advance that this may happen.

I'm writing now to followup about the November 2024 election records. I would like to proceed with my request for those records, assuming they are still available, and am happy to pay the associated fee you outlined in your original response. 

When you have a chance, could you please confirm how I can submit payment to the San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters for the fee of $310.13? Is it only possible to pay through mailed check? Or is there an alternative way to process the payment online? Please let me know what is best. 

Thank you again for your patience, and your most diligent assistance with my request(s).  

All my best,
Aleksandra

Thank you kindly for this information, and for your help with my request. I am trying to make the payment now but I called the number you wrote in our most recent correspondence to make payment (909-387-8300), and I was told that it was not the correct line for making payments.

Please note that precincts with 11 or fewer voters have been redacted from the report, as the information is exempt under the following provisions: (1) Article II, Section 7 of the California Constitution, which guarantees each voter the right to cast a secret ballot; (2) Government Code section 7924.000(a) and the United States Constitution, protecting the secrecy of votes; and (3) Government Code section 7922.000, the public's interest in protecting the secrecy of voters' choices outweighs the public's right to disclosure of small precinct identification. A list of the precincts and the number of voters in each has also been uploaded to Next Request for viewing and download. This concludes our response and the PRA request is now closed. Sincerely, Thalia Amador Elections Specialist * CVR_Export_20260501130004.csv Document links are valid for one month. After June 4, you will need to sign in to view the document(s). ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Ingham County)

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Ingham County)
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

title: Newport News Va  Police partol  staff roster request as of April22,2026

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Newport News Va  Police partol  staff roster request as of April22,2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Date: April 22, 2026

Good afternoon, I hope this email finds you well and safe.

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act (Va. Code § 2.2‑3700 et seq.), I respectfully request a current roster of sworn personnel assigned to standard patrol operations within the Newport News Police Department, including all patrol precincts, beats, and field operations units, but excluding special tactical teams and supervisory ranks, as of April 22, 2026.

For each officer, please include:
- Full name
- Badge number or identifier
- Rank or title
- Patrol precinct, beat, or assignment area
- Unit assignment
- Shift assignment
- Hire date
- K‑9 designation (if applicable)

This request is made for non‑commercial, educational, and research purposes. I am not seeking any confidential personal identifiers or information protected by law. As I am unable to receive mailed records, please email all responsive documents.

Thank you for your time and consideration.
I hope to hear from you soon.

Sincerely,
Cameron Oliver
Jacksonville, FL
Email: oliverkidsjax@gmail.com
Phone: 904‑489‑9118

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Cameron John Oliver.

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Newport News Va Police partol  staff roster request as of April22,2026
communications.communication:
Additional time is needed as it is not practically possible to provide a response within the five working days outlined by FOIA. Therefore, in accordance with Virginia Code § 2.2-3704(B)(4), we will require an additional seven working days beyond the initial five days to respond to your request.

In addition, could you please clarify whether you are looking for just patrol officers, or all officers, i.e., detectives, community outreach, special investigations, recruiting, special operations, etc.

Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Newport News Va  Police partol  staff roster request as of April22,2026
communications.communication:
Subject: Clarification of FOIA Request – Patrol Officers Only

Good afternoon,

Thank you for your response. To clarify my request:

I am seeking information only for sworn personnel assigned to standard patrol operations within the Newport News Police Department. This includes officers assigned to patrol precincts, patrol beats, and regular patrol field operations, but does not include detectives, community outreach, special investigations, recruiting, special operations, traffic, administrative sworn staff, or any other non‑patrol units.

K‑9 officers should be included only if they are assigned to patrol duties.

Supervisory ranks (sergeants, lieutenants, captains, etc.) and special tactical teams should remain excluded as stated in the original request.

Thank you for the clarification opportunity. Please proceed with the request based on this narrowed scope.
I hope to hear from you soon. 
Take care and be well. 
Thank you for your time and  asstistance. 
Please let me know if you have any more questions or concerns about this request. 
I look forward to hearing from you soon. 

Sincerely,
Cameron Oliver
904-489-9118 
Oliverkidsjax@gmail.com 
(on behalf of Tom Nash)

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Newport News Va Police partol  staff roster request as of April22,2026
communications.communication:
Mr. Oliver,

On April 22, 2026 the Newport News Police Department received your Virginia Freedom of Information Act request, and subsequent clarification, wherein you requested “a current roster of sworn personnel assigned to standard patrol operations within the Newport News Police Department, including all patrol precincts, beats, and field operations units, but excluding special tactical teams and supervisory ranks, as of April 22, 2026.” Please find enclosed a list of NNPD Patrol Officers, hire dates, rank or title, and K9 status.

In reference to you request for “patrol precinct, beat, or assignment area, unit assignment, and shift assignment,” the Newport News Police Department has chosen has chosen to exercise the FOIA tactical information exemption that is applicable to the said record, and is withholding release of that information as allowed by Va. Code §§2.2-3706 (B) (5).

title: SF-311 (complete)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Request: SF-311 (complete)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act.  I hereby request the following records:

Copies of all SF-311 forms submitted to NARA from all agencies for all available years.

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities for AND Magazine, MuckRock and Glomar Disclosure and have an open arrangement with each. My articles have been widely read, with some reaching over 100,000 readers. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through the non-profit Internet Archive, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance. Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media. Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category. As such, my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it and making the raw data available and I therefore request that fees be waived.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.


Sincerely,

Emma Best

communications.subject: FOIA Request NGC18-179 (NARA-NGC-2018-000333)
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Best,

As we previously advised you via FOIAonline, we received your Privacy
Act/FOIA request, dated February 19, 2018, received in our office February
20, 2018, and we have assigned your request internal tracking number
NGC18-119 and FOIAOnline tracking number NARA-NGC-2018-000333.  Your
request stated:

To Whom It May Concern: This is a request under the Freedom of Information
Act. I hereby request the following records: Copies of all SF-311 forms
submitted to NARA from all agencies for all available years. As such, my
primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it
and making the raw data available and I therefore request that fees be
waived. The requested documents will be made available to the general
public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the
event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of
the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the
request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM
if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this
matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20
business days, as the statute requires. Sincerely, Emma Best

Your request falls within one of the “unusual circumstances” categories
contemplated by the FOIA, 5 U.S.C. 552 §§ (a)(6)(B)(iii)((I) and (III), and
we need additional time to respond to your request beyond the twenty
business days provided by the FOIA statute.  The records you seek have
required us to search several offices within NARA.  To enable us to respond
more quickly to you, you may wish to reformulate your request to reduce the
amount of time required to complete our consultation, or to arrange an
alternative time frame for the processing of this request.

We regret the necessity of this delay, but assure you that your request
will be processed as soon as possible.  If you have any questions you may
contact me.  You may also contact our FOIA Public Liaison, Gary M. Stern
for assistance at 8601 Adelphi Road, College Park, MD 20740-6002,
301-837-1750, Garym.Stern@nara.gov.

Sincerely,


Matthew Heichelbech
Government Information Specialist Detailee
National Archives and Records Administration
Office of the General Counsel, Archives II
301-837-1934
matthew.heichelbech@nara.gov

<matthew.heichelbech@nara.gov>

<matthew.heichelbech@nara.gov>

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #NARA-NGC-2018-000333, F-00004-19, OIG-2019-00016
communications.communication:
I am appealing USAID's determination that I am an all other requester, as this is blatantly inconsistent with the facts and with case law. I further appeal their determination that I agreed to pay up to $50, which I never did. I further appeal the decision to ignore my fee waiver request. 

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers in outlets such as MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, AND Magazine, Unicorn Riot, and more. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive and and the journalist non-profit, MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.

Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category.

communications.subject: USDA DFO FOIA 2019-DA-00426-F Routing to OIG
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Best:

This e-mail is in follow-up to the October 19, 2018 acknowledgment, by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) Departmental FOIA Office (DFO), of the October 16, 2018 Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) referral to the DFO from the US National Archives and Records Administration (NARA), pertaining to your attached FOIA requests seeking SF-311 records.

The DFO is under the purview of the Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) and serves as the focal point for USDA's FOIA program. The DFO provides coordination and ensures agency-wide compliance with the FOIA. Additionally, the DFO processes requests and appeals on behalf of the Office of the Secretary and Departmental Administration's (DA) components (previously Departmental Management). For a complete listing of DA's components, please visit http://www.dm.usda.gov/agencies.htm.

Following a review of the responsive records referred to DFO by NARA, the DFO determined that the responsive records contain equities of the USDA Office of the Inspector General (OIG). The DFO is referring those records to OIG for processing and direct response to you. In the event you have questions regarding the processing of the responsive records, please contact the OIG FOIA Officer:

Alison Decker
FOIA Officer
1400 Independence Avenue, SW
Room 441-E Whitten Building
Mail Stop 2308
Washington, DC 20250
Tel. 202-720-5677
Fax 202-690-6305
Email: FOIAStaff@oig.usda.gov

For additional information regarding USDA FOIA regulations and frequently requested records, please visit the USDA FOIA homepage at www.dm.usda.gov/foia. There you can access our Public Access Link (PAL). Once registered, PAL allows you to: 1) submit a FOIA request to any of our 20 USDA component agencies electronically; 2) check the status of your FOIA request; and 3) download responsive records as they become available.

If you have any questions or concerns regarding this email, please contact Kathleen Drohan at (202)694-1802, kathleen.drohan@ocio.usda.gov<mailto:kathleen.drohan@ocio.usda.gov>, or USDAFOIA@ocio.usda.gov.

The DFO Team appreciates the opportunity to assist you.
Thanks,

Kathleen Drohan, on behalf of the DFO

Kathleen Drohan

communications.subject: MCC 19-01R Grant Response
communications.communication:
Re: FOIA Request Acknowledgement MCC-FY 19-01R - Grant

Dear Ms. Best:

Please be advised that the Millennium Challenge Corporation (MCC) has granted your request for information pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 U.S.C. §552.  In response to your request dated February 19, 2018 to the National Archives and Records Administration, in which you requested "Copies of all SF-311 forms submitted to NARA from all agencies for all available years," MCC is enclosing two documents responsive to your request. The responsive documents are attached.  If would like to discuss any aspect of your request please do not hesitate to contact our FOIA Program Officer (Acting), Miguel Adams or our FOIA Public Liaison Ann Barnes at FOIA@mcc.gov<mailto:FOIA@mcc.gov>.

Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is as follows:

Office of Government Information Services
National Archives and Records Administration
8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS,
College Park, Maryland 20740-6001
e-mail at ogis@nara.gov
telephone at 202-741-5770
toll free at 1-877-684-6448
facsimile at 202-741-5769.

If you determine that the response to your FOIA request necessitates an appeal, you must initiate your appeal in writing and it must be electronically transmitted or postmarked by MCC within
ninety (90) days after your receipt of this notice. Your appeal must identify the designated reference number (FOIA 19-01R) and include a statement that clearly specifies why you believe an error exists. Your appeal shall be sent to the following address:
Millennium Challenge Corporation
ATTN: FOIA Appeals Officer
1099 Fourteenth Street, NW, 7th Floor
Washington, DC 20005

Miguel Adams
FOIA Program Officer (Acting)
______________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

[cid:image002.jpg@01CFD104.A81B27A0]Millennium Challenge Corporation | 1099 Fourteenth Street NW, Suite 700, Washington, DC 20005-3550 | (O): 202-521-3574 | FAX: 202-521-3947  | adamsmg@mcc.gov<mailto:adamsmg@mcc.gov>

MCC Values are CLEAR:
|Embrace Collaboration | Always Learn | Practice Excellence |Be Accountable | Respect Individuals & Ideas |
See how MCC is reducing poverty through growth, visit us at www.mcc.gov<http://www.mcc.gov/> | P please consider the environment before printing this e-mail

communications.subject: 19-00114-F
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Best;
This is in reference to the above numbered FOIA request that you submitted to this office on 2/19/18. In it you requested the following:

I hereby request the following records: Copies of all drafted, submitted or received SF-311 forms (https://www.archives.gov/files/isoo/security-forms/sf3ll.pdf) produced in the year prior to the receipt of this FOIA request. SF-311 forms may also be identified as Agency Security Classification Management Program Data. I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the Internet Archive and MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience.

On behalf of the Department, please accept my apology for the delay in finalizing this FOIA request, but I'm reaching out to you to determine if you are still interested in having the Department continue to process this request.  Over the passage of time, the need for particular information diminishes, and in our current climate of limited resources, it would be beneficial to know if we no longer need to allocate resources to this request.  Please let me know if you are still interested as soon as possible by responding to this email.  A lack of response by April 9, 2020 will be interpreted that this request is no longer of interest, resulting in its administrative closure.

Again, on behalf of the Department, please accept my apologies for the delay in finalizing your FOIA request.

Thanks in advance for your attention to this matter!

[cid:image001.png@01D5F5F7.F4309710]

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Isabella County)

communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Isabella County)
communications.communication: Hello, can you confirm receipt of my request please?

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Isabella County)
communications.communication:
Good Morning,

Unfortunately, a prior FOIA request was never received by foiaadmin@isabellacounty.org<mailto:foiaadmin@isabellacounty.org>.

I have shared this request with our Equalization Department and they are working on producing the data/information requested: (a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request. Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data).
Please be advised that per the Isabella County Enhanced Access Policy, there is a cost for the data sent to your company and the requested records will be released upon receipt of the appropriate fee of $1,000.00.
Please know that the FOIA fee provisions under MCL 15.234 do not apply where another statute authorizes the sale of the records, or if the amount of the fee for providing a copy of the public record is otherwise specifically
provided by an act or statute. MCL 15.234(10).

Please see the attached Detailed Cost Itemization which includes the Isabella County Equalization fee schedule. Please make check payable to Isabella County and send to the Isabella County Administration Office,
200 N. Main Street, Suite 205, Mt. Pleasant, MI 48858. The data will be released within 5-7 business days, once payment has been received.

Thank you!


Julie McKinley
Executive Assistant
Isabella County Administrator/Controller’s Office
PH: 989-317-4054
FAX: 989-317-4276
jmckinley@isabellacounty.org<mailto:jmckinley@isabellacounty.org>

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Isabella County)
communications.communication:
Hi Julie, thank you for getting back to me with the Isabella Co Cost Sheet.

From the Cost Sheet, it says "records prepared under an act or statute specifically authorizing the sale of those public records to the public" -- could you refer me to the act or statute from which Isabella Co derived the quoted price?

I believe it should have an MCL# (the act or statute being from the state legislature as opposed to a county ordinance or policy which would not have an MCL#).

Thank you!

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Chicopee Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Chicopee Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)

communications.subject: Request# 2026-1025 : Request Submission Acceptance Confirmation
communications.communication:
Please DO NOT reply to this email !! Please use the email address of the sender at bottom of this email, in order to communicate with the request management team. 
City of Framingham, MA 
Public Record Request Number:2026-1025 
Requester: Joshua  Dankoff 
Request Date: Tuesday, March 31, 2026 8:00:00 AM 
Response Due Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 

Dear  Joshua  Dankoff : 

We have received your public records request. This request is being assigned to the appropriate department. The department records access officer will review the request and create an effort estimate to comply with your request where applicable. Pursuant to Massachusetts General Law, if the effort estimate is more than 2 hours, we will make a proposed cost estimate and will email such estimate to your attention. Once you approve the amount and send the payment, we will begin working on your request. 
If the effort estimate is less than 2 hours, we will proceed to work on the response and will send you the response once the work is completed. Please also note that Massachusetts General Law allows us to complete and send the response within 10 business days. However, the response date may be extended for any of the following reasons: 
1.   Any clarification that is required from you towards this request will stop the clock. The clock will be resumed once the proper clarification is received. 
2.   Payment of estimated effort. The clock stops once the estimate is sent out. It will resume only after the estimated amount has been paid. 
3.   In the records access officer's opinion, if it is determined that the original due date cannot be met despite reasonable effort. The due date shall be extended based on statutory regulations. You will be promptly notified in such a case with the revised due date or copied on a petition to the Supervisor of Public Records.

Thank you. 
Colleen Kehew, Department RAOPolice Department 

City of Framingham 
1 William Welch Way 
Framingham, MA 01702 
Tel: (508)-872-1212 
Email: ckehew@framinghamma.gov

title: Franklin County Domestic Court

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Franklin County Domestic Court
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) Any and all emails and Microsoft Teams messages sent or received between March 1, 2026, and the time this request is processed, between any Judge (or their judicial staff) and the Court's Information Technology (IT) department containing the word 'Dispatch'.
2) Any and all emails and Microsoft Teams messages sent or received between March 1, 2026, and the time this request is processed, between any Judge (or their judicial staff) and the Court's Information Technology (IT) department about or related to any non-disclosure agreements. 
3) Copies of agendas, meeting minutes, and meeting notes from any judges' meetings or administrative meetings held between March 1, 2026, and the date this request is processed.
4) Copies of agendas, meeting minutes, and meeting notes from any administrative meetings held between March 1, 2026, and the date this request is processed.
5) The complete administrative human resources and personnel files (including but not limited to hiring documents, disciplinary records, and performance reviews) for Teneica Plair and Terrence Scott.
6) Any administrative, payroll, or internal disciplinary files maintained by the Court for Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, Judge Lasheyl Stroud, and Judge George W. Leach.
7) All drafts, revisions, and the final executed template of the employee Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) implemented in March 2026. Additionally, all meeting notes, internal memoranda, and emails regarding the creation and implementation of this NDA.
8)The electronic access logs and/or keycard swipe data for Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, Judge Lasheyl Stroud, and Judge George W. Leach from March 1, 2025, to the present. To preemptively resolve any 'security record' exemption concerns under Sup.R. 44 or R.C. 149.433, the requester expressly consents to the redaction of all specific timestamps (hours and minutes) and specific physical door/reader locations. The requester is seeking only the number of unredacted days on which each judge's badge was utilized to access the building for the requested time period.
9) Any invoices, billing statements, or contracts for outside legal counsel engaged by the Court to draft, review, or advise on employee Non-Disclosure Agreements during the 2026 calendar year.
10) Any emails, HR memos, or internal communications discussing any court employee's refusal to sign the March 2026 Non-Disclosure Agreement, or discussing disciplinary consequences for failing to sign the agreement.
11) Any IT helpdesk tickets or service requests submitted by, or on behalf of, any domestic court judge, between March 1, 2026, and the date this request is processed, specifically including any requests relating to email retrieval, data deletion, computer wiping, or accessing security camera footage.
12) Any and all emails, including attachments and the full, unredacted recipient lists (To, Cc, and Bcc fields) from March 1, 2026, and the present that contained the blank Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) or instructions for employees to sign the NDA.
13) All vendor invoices, purchase orders, and payment records for any construction, renovation, interior design, or remodeling services performed in the courtroom or judicial chambers assigned to Judge Lasheyl Stroud from January 1, 2023, to the present.
14) All contracts, bids, or proposals from architects, general contractors, or furniture vendors regarding the renovation, remodeling, or refurnishing of Judge Stroud's courtroom and chambers.
15) Any internal emails, memoranda, or budget requests sent by or on behalf of Judge Stroud to the Administrative Judge or the Court Administration regarding funding for the renovation or remodeling of her courtroom and chambers.
16) Any and all internal emails, Microsoft Teams messages, or administrative memoranda sent or received by the Court Director, Legal Director, HR Director, Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, and/or any domestic court judge, discussing, referencing, or attempting to ascertain the identity of the requester of this specific public records request.
17) Any calendar invitations, calendar updates, emails, or Microsoft Teams messages sent or received by Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, the Court Director, any individual Judge, or judicial staff between March 5, 2026, and the time this request is processed, containing the words 'emergency', 'urgent', 'mandatory', or 'special' in reference to the scheduling or convening of a judges' meeting, administrative meeting, or all-staff meeting.
18) Any calendar invitations, calendar updates, emails, or Microsoft Teams messages sent or received by Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, the Court Director, any individual Judge, or judicial staff between March 5, 2026, and the time this request is processed, containing the word “meeting”.
19) Any and all emails, memoranda, drafts, or formal legal opinions exchanged between the Domestic Relations Court (including any Judge, Judicial staff, the Legal Director, or the HR department) and the Franklin County Prosecutor's Office regarding the drafting, legality, or implementation of the March 2026 employee Non-Disclosure Agreement, from January 1, 2026, to the present.
20) Any IT helpdesk tickets, service requests, or communications directed to the Court's Information Technology (IT) department or Franklin County Data Center requesting an IP address trace, email header analysis, domain lookup, or any other technical identity verification regarding the email address submitting this request or the properties of the documents submitted as a part of this request
21) Any and all records, including but not limited to calendar invitations, emails, SMS/text messages, or Microsoft Teams messages, sent or received on March 26, 2026, by Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, the Court Director, or any Judge of the Domestic Relations Division, regarding the scheduling, convening, or agendas for any 'emergency,' 'special,' or 'unscheduled' meeting of the judges or court leadership held on this date and any meeting records or notes from any such meeting. 


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Doe

  1.  The electronic access logs and/or keycard swipe data for Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, Judge Lasheyl Stroud, and Judge George W. Leach from March 1, 2025, to the present. To preemptively resolve any 'security record' exemption concerns under Sup.R. 44 or R.C. 149.433, the requester expressly consents to the redaction of all specific timestamps (hours and minutes) and specific physical door/reader locations. The requester is seeking only the number of unredacted days on which each judge's badge was utilized to access the building for the requested time period.


  1.  All vendor invoices, purchase orders, and payment records for any construction, renovation, interior design, or remodeling services performed in the courtroom or judicial chambers assigned to Judge Lasheyl Stroud from January 1, 2023, to the present.

3.    All contracts, bids, or proposals from architects, general contractors, or furniture vendors regarding the renovation, remodeling, or refurnishing of Judge Stroud's courtroom and chambers.
Please be advised that your request is currently under legal review to ensure compliance with applicable provisions of the Ohio Public Records Act (R.C. 149.43) and other relevant laws. This step is part of our standard process to evaluate whether any exemptions apply and to determine the appropriate response.

We will provide an update as soon as the review is complete. We appreciate your patience during this process.

If you have any questions in the meantime, feel free to contact our office.


Thank you,

communications.subject: RE: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Franklin County Domestic Court
communications.communication:
Good morning,

Thank you for your recent public records request.  Here is a partial release of the records you requested:

The electronic access logs and/or keycard swipe data for Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, Judge Lasheyl Stroud, and Judge George W. Leach from March 1, 2025, to the present. To preemptively resolve any 'security record' exemption concerns under Sup.R. 44 or R.C. 149.433, the requester expressly consents to the redaction of all specific timestamps (hours and minutes) and specific physical door/reader locations. The requester is seeking only the number of unredacted days on which each judge's badge was utilized to access the building for the requested time period.

Redactions were applied pursuant to RC 149.433(A)(1) and 149.433(B)(1) as a record that contains information directly used to protect or maintain the security of a public office is a "security record," and a record that is a security record is not a public record under Ohio Revised Code section 149.43 and is not subject to mandatory release.

"Security record" is any record that "contains information directly used for protecting or maintaining the security of a public office against attack, interference, or sabotage . . . [or] to prevent, mitigate, or respond to acts of terrorism."414 Protecting a public office includes protecting the employees, officers, and agents who work in that office.415 For example, a prison's shift-assignment duty rosters that showed where guards are posted, which guards are assigned to a particular post, and how many will be there at a given time, were exempt as security records. The court said that knowing where and when guards would be posted could be used to plan an escape or attack, or to smuggle contraband into the prison. McDougald v. Greene, 162 Ohio St.3d 250, 2020-Ohio-4268.] State ex rel. Cincinnati Enquirer v. Wilson, 2024-Ohio-182, ¶ 4.

[?Folder icon] PRR_FCCourts_JudgesLogs<https://franklincountyohio.sharepoint.com/:f:/s/BOC-PublicRecords/IgAX3jvZFpMASKVM6z31YA5sASVRX_HMkY_-_dmuKLrc4Io?email=207630-44683051%40requests.muckrock.com&e=wcnXVq>

The remaining two items in your public records request are still being compiled.

All vendor invoices, purchase orders, and payment records for any construction, renovation, interior design, or remodeling services performed in the courtroom or judicial chambers assigned to Judge Lasheyl Stroud from January 1, 2023, to the present.

All contracts, bids, or proposals from architects, general contractors, or furniture vendors regarding the renovation, remodeling, or refurnishing of Judge Stroud's courtroom and chambers.

Please let us know if you have any questions or need further clarification.

Thank you,
Brittany

title: San Rafael Police Department Human Trafficking

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: San Rafael Police Department Human Trafficking
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents produced between January 1st, 2023 until March 16th 2026 that include the term "human trafficking" "Eighteenth Street Gang" "Nortenos" "Mafia" "Nuestra Familia" "homeless" "unhoused" produced by the San Rafael police department. 

All police reports pertaining to human trafficking produced between January 1st, 2023 until March 16th 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robbie Powelson

title: Washington Public Records Act Request: Personnel and Conduct Records (Jefferson County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Washington Public Records Act Request: Personnel and Conduct Records (Jefferson County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, I respectfully request copies of the following records covering the past ten (10) years:

Employee Information:
•	Personnel Rosters: Full list of current and former officers and staff, including names, badge numbers, 
ranks/positions, assignments, hiring dates, lengths of service, and current salaries.
•	Separated Personnel Records: Officer names, badge numbers, ranks/positions, dates of separation, reasons for separation, and any disciplinary or investigation findings related to separated employees.
•	Internal Affairs Complaints Logs: Logs of complaints filed against officers, including dates, nature of complaints, investigation summaries, and outcomes.
•	Demographic Breakdown of Staff: Information regarding race, gender, age, and years of service for all personnel.
•	Disciplinary Records: Records of sustained misconduct, reprimands, suspensions, demotions, and terminations.
•	Citizen Complaint Records: Complaints filed by members of the public, including investigation records and findings.
•	Officer Training Records: Records of completed certifications, dates of training courses, and ongoing education.
•	Use of Force Reports: Reports and records detailing any incidents where physical force was used, including the type of force (firearms, tasers, batons, etc.).
•	Officer-Involved Shooting Reports: Investigative files and administrative reviews regarding officer-involved shootings.
•	Early Intervention System (EIS) Data: Records identifying officers flagged for multiple complaints, use of force incidents, or other concerning behavior.
•	Performance Evaluations: Annual or biannual performance reviews for individual officers.
•	Grievance Records: Labor grievances filed by or against officers.
•	Supervisor Notes and Counseling Records: Informal disciplinary notes and performance counseling documentation.
•	Resignation and Retirement Records: Records detailing reasons for resignations or retirements, especially after critical incidents.
•	Awards and Commendations: Records of any awards, commendations, or honors given to officers.
•	Off-Duty Misconduct Records: Records involving arrests or disciplinary actions related to off-duty behavior.
•	Records of Lawsuits Against Officers: Civil suits alleging officer misconduct, civil rights violations, or other professional conduct matters.
•	Settlement Agreements Involving Officers: Terms and details of any settlement agreements, including any non-disclosure clauses.
•	Brady Lists: Lists or notifications identifying officers with documented credibility issues.
•	Body-Worn Camera Misuse or Policy Violation Records: Instances where officers failed to comply with body-worn camera policies.
•	Records of Transfers and Reassignments: Records regarding officer transfers or reassignments, including reasons where available.
•	Fitness-for-Duty Evaluations: Psychological or medical evaluations required after misconduct allegations or other triggering events.
•	Promotions and Demotions Records: Application materials, evaluation records, and decision memorandums regarding promotions and demotions.
•	Employment Application Materials: Initial applications, resumes, background check summaries (redacted for private information if necessary).
•	Secondary Employment Approvals: Records showing agency approval for secondary employment by officers (e.g., security jobs).
•	Internal Memos Regarding Conduct Policy Changes: Memos or correspondence discussing changes to conduct policies, disciplinary procedures, or related codes.
•	Salary Records: Current and historical salary data for all personnel, including base pay, overtime, special assignment pay, and bonuses.

Format and Fees:
In order of preference, I would prefer that this data be provided as: plain text with comma-separated-values (CSV file), OpenDocument spreadsheet, Excel spreadsheet, or the native electronic format of the software from which this information is retrieved in fulfillment of my request. I specifically request that you not create a new record by rendering to PDF or by printing your electronic record then scanning it to make a series of images of the printed data.

Please produce the records in electronic format (.csv, .xlsx, .ods, etc.) via email or secure download link to avoid unnecessary copying costs.

If the total estimated cost to fulfill this request exceeds $25, please provide a written, itemized cost estimate before proceeding. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.120(2)(b), I request a waiver or reduction of fees as this request is made for the purpose of public interest and community oversight, not for commercial purposes.

If Portions Are Exempt:
If any portions of the requested records are exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation for each redaction or withheld record, citing the specific exemption relied upon.

If Delayed or Denied:
If fulfillment of this request will take longer than the statutory time frame, or if you believe any aspect is overly burdensome, please provide a written explanation identifying specifically which portions are burdensome and propose a timeline for rolling production of records as they become available.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me at [Insert Your Email] if clarification is needed.

Sincerely,

Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003598-042925
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 29, 2025, Reference # S003598-042925 Dear Aurora  Quinn, The County received the following public records request from you on April 29, 2025: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, I respectfully request copies of the following records covering the past ten (10) years: 

Employee Information: 
• Personnel Rosters: Full list of current and former officers and staff, including names, badge numbers, 
ranks/positions, assignments, hiring dates, lengths of service, and current salaries. 
• Separated Personnel Records: Officer names, badge numbers, ranks/positions, dates of separation, reasons for separation, and any disciplinary or investigation findings related to separated employees. 
• Internal Affairs Complaints Logs: Logs of complaints filed against officers, including dates, nature of complaints, investigation summaries, and outcomes. 
• Demographic Breakdown of Staff: Information regarding race, gender, age, and years of service for all personnel. 
• Disciplinary Records: Records of sustained misconduct, reprimands, suspensions, demotions, and terminations. 
• Citizen Complaint Records: Complaints filed by members of the public, including investigation records and findings. 
• Officer Training Records: Records of completed certifications, dates of training courses, and ongoing education. 
• Use of Force Reports: Reports and records detailing any incidents where physical force was used, including the type of force (firearms, tasers, batons, etc.). 
• Officer-Involved Shooting Reports: Investigative files and administrative reviews regarding officer-involved shootings. 
• Early Intervention System (EIS) Data: Records identifying officers flagged for multiple complaints, use of force incidents, or other concerning behavior. 
• Performance Evaluations: Annual or biannual performance reviews for individual officers. 
• Grievance Records: Labor grievances filed by or against officers. 
• Supervisor Notes and Counseling Records: Informal disciplinary notes and performance counseling documentation. 
• Resignation and Retirement Records: Records detailing reasons for resignations or retirements, especially after critical incidents. 
• Awards and Commendations: Records of any awards, commendations, or honors given to officers. 
• Off-Duty Misconduct Records: Records involving arrests or disciplinary actions related to off-duty behavior. 
• Records of Lawsuits Against Officers: Civil suits alleging officer misconduct, civil rights violations, or other professional conduct matters. 
• Settlement Agreements Involving Officers: Terms and details of any settlement agreements, including any non-disclosure clauses. 
• Brady Lists: Lists or notifications identifying officers with documented credibility issues. 
• Body-Worn Camera Misuse or Policy Violation Records: Instances where officers failed to comply with body-worn camera policies. 
• Records of Transfers and Reassignments: Records regarding officer transfers or reassignments, including reasons where available. 
• Fitness-for-Duty Evaluations: Psychological or medical evaluations required after misconduct allegations or other triggering events. 
• Promotions and Demotions Records: Application materials, evaluation records, and decision memorandums regarding promotions and demotions. 
• Employment Application Materials: Initial applications, resumes, background check summaries (redacted for private information if necessary). 
• Secondary Employment Approvals: Records showing agency approval for secondary employment by officers (e.g., security jobs). 
• Internal Memos Regarding Conduct Policy Changes: Memos or correspondence discussing changes to conduct policies, disciplinary procedures, or related codes. 
• Salary Records: Current and historical salary data for all personnel, including base pay, overtime, special assignment pay, and bonuses. 

Format and Fees: 
In order of preference, I would prefer that this data be provided as: plain text with comma-separated-values (CSV file), OpenDocument spreadsheet, Excel spreadsheet, or the native electronic format of the software from which this information is retrieved in fulfillment of my request. I specifically request that you not create a new record by rendering to PDF or by printing your electronic record then scanning it to make a series of images of the printed data. 

Please produce the records in electronic format (.csv, .xlsx, .ods, etc.) via email or secure download link to avoid unnecessary copying costs. 

If the total estimated cost to fulfill this request exceeds $25, please provide a written, itemized cost estimate before proceeding. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.120(2)(b), I request a waiver or reduction of fees as this request is made for the purpose of public interest and community oversight, not for commercial purposes. 

If Portions Are Exempt: 
If any portions of the requested records are exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation for each redaction or withheld record, citing the specific exemption relied upon. 

If Delayed or Denied: 
If fulfillment of this request will take longer than the statutory time frame, or if you believe any aspect is overly burdensome, please provide a written explanation identifying specifically which portions are burdensome and propose a timeline for rolling production of records as they become available. 

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me at [Insert Your Email] if clarification is needed. 

Sincerely, 

Aurora Quinn 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fjefferson-county-sheriffs-office-35220%252Fwashington-public-records-act-request-personnel-and-conduct-records-jefferson-county-sheriffs-office-185736%252F%253Femail%253Djnole%252540co.jefferson.wa.us&url_auth_token=AAGleWFczW3cyV5iQFlhuFOS7Jc%3A1u9x90%3AKCxjEj5LjwQO1vxW4KL6XvlE_tuWhH_Cl1xA8skaZTU 
Due to the extensive amount of information requested some additional time will be needed to locate, compile and review responsive records. We estimate that public records responsive to this request will be available within 60___ business days from today, on or before 8/1/2025. If public records become available in advance of this estimate we will contact you promptly. Conversely, if the County encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you promptly with a revised estimated date. Thank you for your patience in awaiting this information. Sincerely, Lynn Chesterfield 
Support Services Technician 2 
Sheriff's Office 
Jefferson County 
360-385-3831

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003598-042925
communications.communication:
Subject: Request for Clarification Regarding Records Request Timeline

Dear Lynn Chesterfield,

Thank you for your response to my public records request.

I respectfully express concern regarding the 60-business-day estimate provided. A comparable request submitted recently to another regional law enforcement agency was processed with a significantly shorter timeline and a projected fulfillment date of May 27, 2025. This suggests that the records I’ve requested— which are routinely maintained by public agencies— may be reasonably accessible without requiring such an extended delay.

I understand that some of the materials may require review or redaction. However, under RCW 42.56.520, agencies are required to provide the “reasonable estimate” of time necessary to produce records, and I respectfully ask for more detail as to what is contributing to this 60-business-day estimate.

If possible, I also request that responsive records be released on a rolling basis as they become available, rather than in a single, delayed release. I am open to narrowing or prioritizing specific sections of my request to assist in timely fulfillment.

Thank you again for your time and attention. I look forward to your response and hope to work collaboratively to ensure the timely release of these important public records.

Sincerely,

Aurora Quinn

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 29, 2025, Reference # S003598-042925 Dear Aurora  Quinn, The County received the following public records request from you on April 29, 2025: To Whom It May Concern: 
Pursuant to the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, I respectfully request copies of the following records covering the past ten (10) years: 
Employee Information: 
1. Personnel Rosters: Full list of current and former officers and staff, including names, badge numbers, 
ranks/positions, assignments, hiring dates, lengths of service, and current salaries. 
2. Separated Personnel Records: Officer names, badge numbers, ranks/positions, dates of separation, reasons for separation, and any disciplinary or investigation findings related to separated employees. 
3. Internal Affairs Complaints Logs: Logs of complaints filed against officers, including dates, nature of complaints, investigation summaries, and outcomes. 
4. Demographic Breakdown of Staff: Information regarding race, gender, age, and years of service for all personnel. 
5. Disciplinary Records: Records of sustained misconduct, reprimands, suspensions, demotions, and terminations. 
6. Citizen Complaint Records: Complaints filed by members of the public, including investigation records and findings. 
7. Officer Training Records: Records of completed certifications, dates of training courses, and ongoing education. 
8. Use of Force Reports: Reports and records detailing any incidents where physical force was used, including the type of force (firearms, tasers, batons, etc.). 
9. Officer-Involved Shooting Reports: Investigative files and administrative reviews regarding officer-involved shootings. 
10. Early Intervention System (EIS) Data: Records identifying officers flagged for multiple complaints, use of force incidents, or other concerning behavior. 
11. Performance Evaluations: Annual or biannual performance reviews for individual officers. 
12. Grievance Records: Labor grievances filed by or against officers. 
13. Supervisor Notes and Counseling Records: Informal disciplinary notes and performance counseling documentation. 
14. Resignation and Retirement Records: Records detailing reasons for resignations or retirements, especially after critical incidents. 
15. Awards and Commendations: Records of any awards, commendations, or honors given to officers. 
16. Off-Duty Misconduct Records: Records involving arrests or disciplinary actions related to off-duty behavior. 
17. Records of Lawsuits Against Officers: Civil suits alleging officer misconduct, civil rights violations, or other professional conduct matters. 
18. Settlement Agreements Involving Officers: Terms and details of any settlement agreements, including any non-disclosure clauses. 
19. Brady Lists: Lists or notifications identifying officers with documented credibility issues. 
20. Body-Worn Camera Misuse or Policy Violation Records: Instances where officers failed to comply with body-worn camera policies. 
21. Records of Transfers and Reassignments: Records regarding officer transfers or reassignments, including reasons where available. 
22. Fitness-for-Duty Evaluations: Psychological or medical evaluations required after misconduct allegations or other triggering events. 
23. Promotions and Demotions Records: Application materials, evaluation records, and decision memorandums regarding promotions and demotions. 
24. Employment Application Materials: Initial applications, resumes, background check summaries (redacted for private information if necessary). 
25. Secondary Employment Approvals: Records showing agency approval for secondary employment by officers (e.g., security jobs). 
26. Internal Memos Regarding Conduct Policy Changes: Memos or correspondence discussing changes to conduct policies, disciplinary procedures, or related codes. 
27. Salary Records: Current and historical salary data for all personnel, including base pay, overtime, special assignment pay, and bonuses. 
I will be coordinating your records request with the Jefferson County Sheriff's Office (JCSO). My apologies for the very long estimate that was initially sent to you. I was out of town and one of the records staff at JCSO sent that estimate based on their perception of the size of your request. We do actually intend to produce records by installment as records are located, compiled and review is completed. A few of your request items are relatively simple and can correspondingly be produced relatively quickly. However, unless I'm misunderstanding any details, the overall scope of your request appears to be massive. I have taken the liberty of numbering each of the bulleted items in your request, for ease of reference. I would guess that 27 of your requests, taken together, comprise a significant portion, probably a majority, of all of JCSO's administrative records. Many of the requested records will require detailed review and redaction which is a time-consuming process. Some of the requested records will also require third party notice to be sent to subjects of the records, which will also add to the overall processing time for those records. Given limitations in personnel and the need to prevent excessive interference with other essential operations, it will take a period of many months to complete all 27 items of your request. But, as I mentioned, we will produce records by installment as they are compiled, prepared and reviewed. Some of the records you are requesting exist in paper files only. The county charges 10 cents a page for scans of paper records, so there will be some charges for certain installments along the way. The total cost of all charges will almost certainly be well in excess of $25. Each time we anticipate charges for a certain installment, we will first send you an estimate of the charges, showing an estimate of the number of pages to be scanned. We estimate that public records responsive to this request will be available within nine business days from today, on or before 5/30/2025. If public records become available in advance of this estimate we will contact you promptly. Conversely, if the County encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you promptly with a revised estimated date. Thank you for your patience in awaiting this information. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003598-042925
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Hugoniot,

Thank you for your detailed explanation regarding the scope and complexity of my public records request. I appreciate your transparency about the challenges involved, including the time required for review, redaction, and third-party notifications, as well as the potential costs associated with scanning paper records.
Understanding the broad nature of my request, I want to be mindful of limiting costs and minimizing any operational burden on Jefferson County. To that end, I would be happy to work with you to identify an initial set of 10–15 high-priority categories that can be compiled and released earlier in the process. If you could advise which of the requested items are more readily accessible or less resource-intensive to produce, that would help me focus the request accordingly. Additionally, I would appreciate it if you could provide a simple tracking update or log indicating the current status of each category as they move through review and preparation. This would help me monitor progress and plan accordingly.

Thank you again for your willingness to produce records on a rolling basis and for providing advance estimates of any anticipated charges. I look forward to receiving the first installment of records by May 30, 2025, or sooner if available, and to working collaboratively toward a transparent and timely resolution.

Sincerely,

Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003598-042925
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Hello Aurora, 
We have reviewed each of the items in your request and identified a few items which can be completed expeditiously. We have also identified some other items for which we have either no responsive records or very few responsive records. Unless you have any objection, we will process those requests first and then move on to larger and more time-consuming requests. The items we identified for this "first round" are numbers 
1, 3, 4, 10, 12, 13, 16, 17, 19, and 20. 
You asked for something like a tracking update or log to track the progress of each item.  The best way to approach this is to create a new request in our public records request system for each item when we begin working on it. These will be essentially sub-requests. The sub-requests will give both you and us an easy way to track the progress of each item. All of the records produced for an item will be stored in that item's sub-request and provided via the sub-request. Also all communication regarding each item, including time estimates for providing installments of records can also be conducted via the corresponding sub-request. All of the sub-requests will appear as individual requests in your account and each of them will have its own reference number that gets generated when it's entered. I'll be creating a few of these sub-requests today and you'll get an automatic acknowledgement email from each one as it's created. 
Please let me know if you have any questions or objections about any of this. 
Thank you, Ken Hugoniot 
Jefferson County Public Records Administrator 
1820 Jefferson St. 
Port Townsend, WA 98368 
(360) 385-9174 
khugoniot@co.jefferson.wa.us (mailto:khugoniot@co.jefferson.wa.us)

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003644-052225 (20) Body-Worn Camera Misuse or Policy Violation Records: Instances where officers failed to comply with body-worn camera policies. Dear Aurora  Quinn, JCSO has determined that there are no responsive records for this request. JCSO began its body-worn camera program in 2022 and there have been no instances of body-worn camera misuse or policy violations since that time. 
This is the final, definitive response from JCSO to your request. This response completes and closes out Jefferson County Public Records Request #S003644-052225. JCSO will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so within 10 business days. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. Please include your name, address, and a copy of this letter together with a brief statement identifying the basis of the appeal. 

Please submit your appeal via GovQA, the mail, or deliver your appeal to: 
Jefferson County Sheriff’s Office 
Attn: Public Records/Front Desk 
79 Elkins Rd. 
Port Hadlock, WA 98339 Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County

communications.subject: Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003656-052925
communications.communication:
Dear Aurora  Quinn: 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Jefferson County Sheriff's Office. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on April 30, 2025 and given the reference number S003656-052925 for tracking purposes. 
 
Records Requested: (2) Separated Personnel Records: Officer names, badge numbers, ranks/positions, dates of separation, reasons for separation, and any disciplinary or investigation findings related to separated employees. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant Sheriff department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The Public Records Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed.   Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office 
 If you do not see an acknowledgement/Confirmation email from jeffersoncountywa@mycusthelp.net please check your junk mail. You may need to add jeffersoncountywa@mycusthelp.net to your safe senders list.  Communications with Jefferson County in regards to your public records request will come from this email address.

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003639-052225
communications.communication:
Subject: Refining Scope of Records Requests Before Proceeding

Dear Mr. Hugoniot,

Thank you for your earlier responses regarding my records requests. Before proceeding with payment or next steps, I would like to take time to refine the scope of the requested records to ensure clarity and reduce unnecessary time and cost for both parties.

I am currently reviewing the following 27 requested items and will be preparing a refined, tiered list identifying:
•	Which items apply to current employees only vs. former employees (and how many years back, e.g., last 3–10 years),
•	Which apply specifically to sworn officers vs. administrative staff,
•	Which categories should be prioritized first for delivery.

I also acknowledge that you have already provided:
•	The personnel roster of current employees (Item #1) via the Muckrock portal,
•	A formal response indicating no responsive records for Item #10 (Early Intervention System Data),
•	A formal response indicating no responsive records for Item #20 (Body-Worn Camera Misuse or Policy Violations).

While I reserve the right to revisit these items or seek administrative appeal later, if necessary, I will focus for now on refining the scope of the remaining requests. Please also note that I intend to process all communications and records delivery solely through the Muckrock portal to keep everything centralized. Once I finalize and submit the refined list, I will follow up regarding payment arrangements and next steps.

Thank you for your cooperation. I’ll be back in touch with you shortly.

Sincerely,

Aurora Quinn

Dear Aurora  Quinn, Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (4) Demographic Breakdown of Staff: Information regarding race, gender, age, and years of service for all personnel. 
Jefferson County has located records responsive to your request, which are being released to you electronically. The reports provided in response to this records request were produced by the county human resources department. At the beginning of 2022, the county migrated personnel data to a new database. There is one file containing information in the new database, from 2022 to the present. There are three other reports that capture data from the retired pre-2022 legacy database (GEMS). One of those reports (EEO4-GEMS_2015-2022) was generated for this records request from data that remains from the legacy database. The other two reports were previously generated in the legacy database in 2017 and 2015 and were located among files maintained by human resources. All four reports together represent all files and database information that are responsive to this request. Please log in to the Public Records Center at the following link to retrieve your responsive documents: Sheriff Public Records Request - S003641-052225 All responsive records have been produced and the request is now closed. This completes, closes, and provides Jefferson County's final, definitive response to this request. If you have any difficulty retrieving your documents, please contact me for assistance as soon as possible. Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so at your earliest opportunity. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550.
