communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003639-052225
communications.communication:
Subject: Refined Public Records Request – Tier 1 Items (Clarification and Follow-Up)

Dear Mr. Hugoniot,

Thank you again for your responses to my previous public records request. As part of the clarification and refinement process, I am writing to follow up only on the Tier 1 items that were identified for refinement. These items include: 1, 3, 4, 10, 12, 13, 16, 17, 19, and 20. My goal is to clarify these requests so they can be processed in a manner consistent with Jefferson County’s records practices and Washington’s Public Records Act. Additionally, I want to clarify that I am not using my personal email address for correspondence. All communications should continue through the MuckRock portal, where this request is being formally tracked.

Please see my refined requests below:

Item 1 – Personnel Rosters (Refined Request)
I received a spreadsheet labeled:
•	Personnel_Roster.Current_Employees
However, this request has not been fully fulfilled because:
•	Hire dates are missing for all current employees.
•	Official position descriptions are missing for all listed positions.
Please provide:
1.	The hire date for each individual listed in the current employee roster (this field is currently blank).
2.	The official position description for each unique rank/position title listed (e.g., Patrol Sergeant, Corrections Officer, Support Services Tech I/II, Animal Control Officer, etc.).
Position descriptions may be provided in the format used by Jefferson County Human Resources for job classifications or duty statements.

Item 3 – Internal Affairs Complaints Logs (Refined Request)
Please provide logs or records of complaints filed against sworn officers from January 1, 2015 to the present, including:
•	Date of complaint
•	Name(s) of officer(s) involved
•	Badge number(s)
•	Nature/type of complaint
•	Summary of allegations
•	Status and outcome
•	Date investigation closed
•	Disciplinary action taken (if any)
This applies to both current and former sworn officers employed during the time period. Records may be provided in spreadsheet or narrative form; whichever format includes all the above fields.

Item 4 – Demographic Breakdown of Staff (Refined Request)
Please provide a demographic breakdown of all current employees of the Jefferson County Sheriff’s Office, including:
•	Race/ethnicity
•	Gender
•	Age or age range
•	Rank/position title
Years of service may be included optionally. A summary table or anonymized dataset is acceptable if required for privacy.

Item 10 – Early Intervention System (EIS) Data (Clarification Request)
You stated there are no responsive records and that this is the “final, definitive response.”
To clarify, I am requesting:
1.	Whether Jefferson County Sheriff’s Office has ever maintained an Early Intervention System (EIS) or similar mechanism for identifying concerning officer behavior.
2.	Whether any policies, communications, or evaluations exist relating to EIS or comparable oversight systems.
3.	Confirmation that no such system or record has existed during the past 10 years, if applicable.
4.	A description of the search process used to determine no responsive records exist.

Item 12 – Grievance Records (Refined Request)
Please provide formal grievance records filed by or against current or former sworn personnel in the past 10 years, including:
•	Date of grievance
•	Nature of grievance
•	Names or job titles of parties involved
•	Investigation summaries (if available)
•	Final outcomes
A summary log is acceptable, but please include any related documents if maintained.

Item 13 – Supervisor Notes and Counseling Records (Refined Request)
Please provide supervisor notes or informal counseling records for current and former sworn personnel from the past 10 years, including:
•	Informal warnings
•	Supervisor memos or notes
•	Performance improvement plans (PIPs) not tied to formal discipline
•	Coaching documentation
If these records are not systematically maintained, please indicate so.

Item 16 – Off-Duty Misconduct Records (Refined Request)
Please provide records from the past 10 years involving off-duty misconduct by current or former sworn personnel, including:
•	Incident reports
•	Internal/external investigation documentation
•	Arrests, citations, or disciplinary findings
This includes off-duty conduct that triggered internal review or formal findings.

Item 17 – Records of Lawsuits Against Officers (Refined Request)
Please provide records of any lawsuits filed in the past 10 years involving current or former sworn personnel, including:
•	Officer names (if available)
•	Case summaries and allegations
•	Court docket numbers or identifiers
•	Final outcomes (settled, dismissed, adjudicated)
•	Any internal documentation tied to the case
If full case files are extensive, an index or summary table with references is sufficient.

Item 19 – Brady/Giglio List Records (Refined Request)
Please provide records from the past 10 years identifying current or former sworn personnel who:
•	Were flagged for credibility concerns
•	Were placed on a Brady or Giglio list
•	Were subject to notifications involving dishonesty or bias
This includes:
•	Notices to/from prosecutors
•	Internal documentation or summaries
•	Any determinations related to Brady/Giglio obligations
If the Sheriff’s Office does not maintain a Brady list internally, please confirm whether any correspondence or determinations exist from the Prosecutor’s Office.

Item 20 – Body-Worn Camera Misuse or Policy Violations (Refined Request)
You stated that there are no responsive records and that there have been no violations since the body-worn camera program began in 2022.
To ensure clarity and accountability, please provide:
1.	Any records of complaints, investigations, or disciplinary actions regarding body-worn camera misuse since 2022.
2.	Documentation of the search process used to confirm no records exist, including:
•	The staff members involved in the search
•	The methods and date(s) of the search
•	Any applicable policies related to BWC misuse
If no responsive records truly exist, that is understood, but I am requesting verification of the basis for the determination.

Please confirm receipt of this refined request and feel free to contact me via the MuckRock portal if you require clarification. Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,

Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003639-052225
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — Refined Tier 2 and Tier 3 Items

Dear Mr. Hugoniot,

Thank you again for your assistance with my ongoing public records request. Following up on our prior communications, please find below the refined requests for Tier 2 and Tier 3 items. These refinements are intended to clarify exactly which records I am seeking, to help ensure the requests can be processed efficiently and in accordance with Jefferson County’s records practices and Washington’s Public Records Act.

Please note the following important points:
•	Some records may require third-party review or employee sign-off, which could delay processing.
•	You are welcome to release records as they become available or to reorder and prioritize the tiering as needed.
•	To help keep costs low and avoid unnecessary charges, please provide an itemized estimate of fees before incurring any costs.

Please see my refined requests below:

Tier 2 — Refined Requests

Item 5 – Disciplinary Records
Please provide records of any sustained misconduct findings for current and former sworn officers, including reprimands, suspensions, demotions, and terminations, with documentation of the nature of misconduct and disciplinary decisions.

Item 6 – Citizen Complaint Records
Please provide complaints filed by the public against sworn officers from January 1, 2015, to present, including investigation records, findings, and any resulting disciplinary actions. Applies to current and former sworn officers.

Item 7 – Officer Training Records
Please provide detailed training records for all current sworn officers, including certifications, course names, completion dates, and ongoing or refresher education.

Item 8 – Use of Force Reports
Please provide all reports documenting use of force incidents by sworn officers from January 1, 2015, to present, including type of force, dates, officer names and badge numbers, and circumstances. Includes current and former sworn officers.

Item 9 – Officer-Involved Shooting Reports
Please provide investigative files and administrative reviews related to officer-involved shootings, including internal and external investigations and outcomes, for current and former sworn officers.

Item 11 – Performance Evaluations
Please provide performance evaluations for current and former sworn officers, including narrative comments, ratings, and recommendations.

Item 14 – Resignation and Retirement Records
Please provide records detailing reasons for resignations or retirements of sworn officers, especially related to critical incidents or disciplinary matters, including dates and accompanying documentation.

Item 15 – Awards and Commendations
Please provide records of awards, commendations, or honors given to sworn officers, including dates, types, and reasons for recognition.

Tier 3 — Refined Requests

Item 18 – Settlement Agreements Involving Officers
Please provide full terms and details of settlement agreements involving sworn officers, including any confidentiality or non-disclosure provisions.

Item 21 – Records of Transfers and Reassignments
Please provide records regarding transfers or reassignments of sworn officers, including reasons and effective dates, for current and former sworn officers.

Item 22 – Fitness-for-Duty Evaluations
Please provide psychological or medical evaluation records conducted as fitness-for-duty assessments triggered by misconduct or other events for sworn officers.

Item 23 – Promotions and Demotions Records
Please provide application materials, evaluation records, and decision memorandums related to promotions and demotions of sworn officers, including dates and justifications.

Item 24 – Employment Application Materials
Please provide initial employment applications, resumes, and background check summaries for sworn officers only, redacted as necessary for privacy.

Item 25 – Secondary Employment Approvals
Please provide records showing agency approval for secondary employment by sworn officers, including application dates and approval status.

Item 26 – Internal Memos Regarding Conduct Policy Changes
Please provide memos or correspondence discussing changes to conduct policies, disciplinary procedures, or codes of conduct.

Item 27 – Salary Records
Please provide current and historical salary data for sworn officers, including base pay, overtime, special assignment pay, and bonuses.

Please confirm receipt of this refined request. I appreciate your time and assistance. Feel free to contact me through the MuckRock portal if you require clarification.

Sincerely,

Aurora Quinn

Thank you, Ken Hugoniot 
Jefferson County Public Records Administrator 
1820 Jefferson St.

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003639-052225
communications.communication:
Subject: Follow-Up on Refined Public Records Requests (Tiers 1, 2, and 3)

Dear Mr. Hugoniot,

Thank you for your response and for explaining your internal formatting concerns. For clarity and efficiency, I am submitting a clean, easy-to-read version of my refined Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 requests directly through this MuckRock portal.

Please note that my refined requests were formally submitted through MuckRock on June 15, 2025 (Tier 1) and June 16, 2025 (Tiers 2 and 3). Under RCW 42.56, submission through MuckRock satisfies the requirement for making a public records request, and I expect the response timelines under the Public Records Act to be based on those submission dates.

While I appreciate your preference for alternate formatting, this formal submission through MuckRock should be sufficient for processing. Please confirm that processing is underway and that the agency’s statutory obligations are being met.

Below are the refined requests:


Tier 1 – Refined Requests

Item 1 – Personnel Rosters
Please provide:

1. The hire date for each individual listed in the current employee roster.
2. The official position description for each unique rank/position title listed (e.g., Patrol Sergeant, Corrections Officer, Support Services Tech I/II, Animal Control Officer, etc.).

Item 3 – Internal Affairs Complaints Logs
Please provide logs or records of complaints filed against sworn officers from January 1, 2015 to the present, including:

* Date of complaint
* Name(s) of officer(s) involved
* Badge number(s)
* Nature/type of complaint
* Summary of allegations
* Status and outcome
* Date investigation closed
* Disciplinary action taken (if any)

Item 4 – Demographic Breakdown of Staff
Please provide a demographic breakdown of all current employees, including:

* Race/ethnicity
* Gender
* Age or age range
* Rank/position title

Item 10 – Early Intervention System (EIS) Data
Please provide:

1. Whether the office has ever maintained an Early Intervention System or similar mechanism.
2. Whether any policies, communications, or evaluations exist related to this.
3. Confirmation if no such system or record has existed in the past 10 years.
4. A description of the search process used to determine no responsive records exist.

Item 12 – Grievance Records
Please provide formal grievance records filed by or against current or former sworn personnel in the past 10 years, including:

* Date of grievance
* Nature of grievance
* Names or job titles of involved parties
* Investigation summaries
* Final outcomes

Item 13 – Supervisor Notes and Counseling Records
Please provide supervisor notes or informal counseling records for current and former sworn personnel from the past 10 years, including:

* Informal warnings
* Supervisor memos or notes
* PIPs not tied to formal discipline
* Coaching documentation

Item 16 – Off-Duty Misconduct Records
Please provide records from the past 10 years involving off-duty misconduct by current or former sworn personnel, including:

* Incident reports
* Internal/external investigation documentation
* Arrests, citations, or disciplinary findings

Item 17 – Records of Lawsuits Against Officers
Please provide records of any lawsuits filed in the past 10 years involving current or former sworn personnel, including:

* Officer names (if available)
* Case summaries and allegations
* Court docket numbers
* Final outcomes
* Any internal documentation tied to the case

Item 19 – Brady/Giglio List Records
Please provide records from the past 10 years identifying current or former sworn personnel who:

* Were flagged for credibility concerns
* Were placed on a Brady or Giglio list
* Were subject to dishonesty/bias notifications

Item 20 – Body-Worn Camera Misuse or Policy Violations
Please provide:

1. Any records of complaints, investigations, or disciplinary actions regarding misuse since 2022.
2. Documentation of the search process used to confirm no records exist.


Tier 2 – Refined Requests

Item 5 – Disciplinary Records
Please provide records of sustained misconduct findings for current and former sworn officers, including reprimands, suspensions, demotions, terminations, nature of misconduct, and disciplinary decisions.

Item 6 – Citizen Complaint Records
Please provide complaints filed by the public against sworn officers from January 1, 2015, to present, including investigation records, findings, and any disciplinary actions.

Item 7 – Officer Training Records
Please provide detailed training records for all current sworn officers, including certifications, course names, completion dates, and refresher education.

Item 8 – Use of Force Reports
Please provide reports documenting use of force by sworn officers from January 1, 2015, to present, including type of force, dates, officer names/badge numbers, circumstances.

Item 9 – Officer-Involved Shooting Reports
Please provide investigative files and administrative reviews of officer-involved shootings for current/former sworn officers.

Item 11 – Performance Evaluations
Please provide performance evaluations for current and former sworn officers, including comments, ratings, recommendations.

Item 14 – Resignation and Retirement Records
Please provide records detailing reasons for resignations or retirements of sworn officers, especially related to critical incidents.

Item 15 – Awards and Commendations
Please provide records of awards or honors given to sworn officers, including dates, types, reasons.


Tier 3 – Refined Requests

Item 18 – Settlement Agreements Involving Officers
Please provide terms and details of settlements involving sworn officers, including confidentiality provisions.

Item 21 – Records of Transfers and Reassignments
Please provide records of transfers or reassignments of sworn officers, including reasons and effective dates.

Item 22 – Fitness-for-Duty Evaluations
Please provide fitness-for-duty evaluation records for sworn officers.

Item 23 – Promotions and Demotions Records
Please provide application materials, evaluations, decision memos related to promotions/demotions, dates, justifications.

Item 24 – Employment Application Materials
Please provide initial employment applications, resumes, background summaries for sworn officers.

Item 25 – Secondary Employment Approvals
Please provide records showing agency approval for secondary employment by sworn officers.

Item 26 – Internal Memos Regarding Conduct Policy Changes
Please provide memos or correspondence about changes to conduct policies or disciplinary procedures.

Item 27 – Salary Records
Please provide current and historical salary data for sworn officers, including base pay, overtime, bonuses.


Thank you for your time and assistance. Please confirm receipt of this refined request through this MuckRock portal, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

Sincerely,

Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: Re: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003639-052225
communications.communication:
Hello Aurora,

Thank you for sending this formatted version of your clarifications. For the items we have already been working on, we will update or replace the text in the individual requests we've created for each respective item. We will also provide further communication and records via the same individual requests in our GovQA system. We will give priority to these items and provide more information, records, or time estimates for each within the next few days.

For requests that we have not yet started working on, we will respond to the updated and refined information you've provided.

If we have questions or need for any further communication pertaining to the entire scope of your request (in general, not specific items) then I will communicate those to you from this email address.

Sincerely,


Ken Hugoniot

Jefferson County Public Records Administrator

1820 Jefferson St.

Port Townsend, WA 98368

(360) 385-9174

khugoniot@co.jefferson.wa.us

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003639-052225. Dear Aurora  Quinn, Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (1) Personnel Rosters: Full list of current and former officers and staff, including names, badge numbers, 
ranks/positions, assignments, hiring dates, lengths of service, and current salaries. 
The official position description for each unique rank/position title listed (e.g., Patrol Sergeant, Corrections Officer, Support Services Tech I/II, Animal Control Officer, etc.). 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office has located records responsive to your request, which are being released to you electronically. We have provided all official job descriptions for JCSO. Please note that the job title, "Detective", is included within the "Deputy Sheriff" description and the job title, "Detective Sergeant" is included within the Sergeant of Patrol description. The Clerk Hires are part time employees and there are no official job descriptions for their positions. You also mentioned that we had not included hire dates for current employees. However, hire dates were included in the current employees personnel roster that we provided earlier. Please log in to the Public Records Center at the following link to retrieve your responsive documents: Sheriff Public Records Request - S003639-052225  All responsive records have been produced and the request is now closed. This completes, closes, and provides Jefferson County's final, definitive response to this request. If you have any difficulty retrieving your documents, please contact me for assistance as soon as possible. Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so at your earliest opportunity. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003642-052225
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003642-052225 Dear Aurora  Quinn, The County received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (19) Brady/Giglio List Records 
Please provide records from the past 10 years identifying current or former sworn personnel who: 
* Were flagged for credibility concerns 
* Were placed on a Brady or Giglio list 
* Were subject to dishonesty/bias notifications 
The first installment of records responsive to your request is available to you now in the Jefferson County Public Records Center. The Jefferson County Prosecuting Attorney's Office (PAO) provided two versions of their Brady list. The list in PDF format is the currently used list which is more up to date. The Excel spreadsheet version is the previously-used version which is no longer kept up to date, but it contains some information that is not included in the current list. The PAO issues potential impeachment disclosure (PID) letters pertaining to both Jefferson County Sheriff officers and Port Townsend Police Department officers, so those are included in the responsive records. Please log in at the following link to retrieve your responsive records: Sheriff Public Records Request - S003642-052225  These may be all of the responsive records, but it appears that there is at least one PID letter missing. I will need to inquire with the PAO regarding any remaining unlocated records. Meanwhile, if you have any additional questions or issues pertaining to the records produced in this installment, please don't hesitate to contact me. If any additional records are located, we estimate the next installment to this request will be available within five business days from today, on or before 7/11/2025. If you experience any difficulties accessing the Public Records Center or downloading your documents, please contact me as soon as possible for assistance. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003640-052225. Dear Aurora  Quinn, Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (3) Internal Affairs Complaints Logs 
Please provide logs or records of complaints filed against sworn officers from January 1, 2015 to the present, including: 
* Date of complaint 
* Name(s) of officer(s) involved 
* Badge number(s) 
* Nature/type of complaint 
* Summary of allegations 
* Status and outcome 
* Date investigation closed 
* Disciplinary action taken (if any) 
In response to your records request, Jefferson County Sheriff's Office has provided the full internal affairs log that is maintained by their office. The log includes all types of internal affairs incidents combined in one table. In addition to complaints the log includes use of force incidents, pursuits, workplace injuries and equipment/vehicle damage incidents. The log does not include: 
Badge number 
Date investigation closed The disposition column contains a small amount of information about disciplinary action taken for investigations that were upheld, but the information doesn't appear to be entirely consistent. For current employees, badge numbers can be located by cross-referencing employee names from the IA log with names in the current employee roster, which I've included in the downloads with the IA log file. Since the records of all investigations will be provided to you in response to other items in your overall records request, those documents will include all the details of the investigations, including the date that the investigations were closed and any disciplinary action that was taken. Please log in to the Public Records Center at the following link to retrieve your responsive documents: Sheriff Public Records Request - S003640-052225  All responsive records have been produced and the request is now closed. This completes, closes, and provides Jefferson County's final, definitive response to this request. If you have any difficulty retrieving your documents, please contact me for assistance as soon as possible. Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so at your earliest opportunity. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003656-052925 Dear Aurora  Quinn, The County received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (2) Separated Personnel Records: Officer names, badge numbers, ranks/positions, dates of separation, reasons for separation, and any disciplinary or investigation findings related to separated employees. 
A spreadsheet report that is responsive to your request is available to you now in the Jefferson County Public Records Center. It does not include all of the information you've requested. There is no summary-type of information (such as would be included in a spreadsheet) regarding disciplinary or investigation findings related to employee separations. The most concise responsive records for that would be any investigation reports related to separations. Those reports would also be responsive to item #14, so we can provide them in response to item 2 or item 14, or both. Please let me know your preference. Once I receive a reply from you I can update you on the status of both items. The spreadsheet we provided does not include badge numbers. Badge numbers assigned to former employees are eligible for reuse and can be assigned to new employees. The Jefferson County sheriff's office does not maintain an updated list of badge numbers of former employees. Please log in at the following link to retrieve your responsive records: Sheriff Public Records Request - S003656-052925  Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

Please provide records from the past 10 years identifying current or former sworn personnel who: 
* Were flagged for credibility concerns

communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003644-052225. Dear Aurora  Quinn, Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (20) Body-Worn Camera Misuse or Policy Violations.  Instances where officers failed to comply with body-worn camera policies. 
Please provide: 
1. Any records of complaints, investigations, or disciplinary actions regarding misuse since 2022. 
2. Documentation of the search process used to confirm no records exist. 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office (JCSO) has located records responsive to your request, which are being released to you electronically. The internal affairs files and log were searched, including a key term search of the IA log, and one case was located in which an officer failed to comply with body-worn camera policy. This is IA case #23-01 (complaint date: 01/16/23), related to Deputy Dotson, for failure to activate his BWC and complete CAD notes. JCSO has provided all records related to this case. There is one other IA case (#22-31; complaint date: 09/28/22), pertaining to a BWC video-related policy violation by an employee in the office records staff. The description of the violation in the IA log is, "view BWC footage for private use and share info to public". A letter of reprimand was sent to the employee. Since the employee involved is not an officer, we have not searched for records associated with this case. But we would be happy to conduct a search for those records and provide them to you if you want them. Please advise. Please log in to the Public Records Center at the following link to retrieve your responsive documents: Sheriff Public Records Request - S003644-052225  All responsive records have been produced and the request is now closed. This completes, closes, and provides Jefferson County's final, definitive response to this request. If you have any difficulty retrieving your documents, please contact me for assistance as soon as possible. Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so at your earliest opportunity. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003657-052925 (7) Officer Training Records: Please provide detailed training records for all current sworn officers, including certifications, course names, completion dates, and refresher education. 
Dear Aurora  Quinn, The remaining responsive records for this request are paper-only files, containing training certificates and other training-related records for each officer. The Sheriff's Office has estimated that there are a total of around 5,000 pages of training-related records in these files. Those records would need to be scanned in order to provide them to you. As authorized by RCW 42.56.120(2)(a)(ii), Jefferson County charges ten cents per page for scanned copies. We therefore estimate that it would cost an amount of around $500 to scan and produce the entirety of these records. In response to this estimate, please advise us as to how you would like to proceed with this request item. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator

Jefferson County

communications.subject: Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003814-072825
communications.communication:
Dear Aurora  Quinn: 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Jefferson County Sheriff's Office. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on April 29, 2025 and given the reference number S003814-072825 for tracking purposes. 
 
Records Requested: (16) Off-Duty Misconduct Records
Please provide records from the past 10 years involving off-duty misconduct by current or former sworn personnel, including:

* Incident reports
* Internal/external investigation documentation
* Arrests, citations, or disciplinary findings 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant Sheriff department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The Public Records Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed.   Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office 
 If you do not see an acknowledgement/Confirmation email from jeffersoncountywa@mycusthelp.net please check your junk mail. You may need to add jeffersoncountywa@mycusthelp.net to your safe senders list.  Communications with Jefferson County in regards to your public records request will come from this email address.

Please log in to the Public Records Center at the following link to retrieve your responsive documents: Sheriff Public Records Request - S003640-052225  All responsive records have been produced and the request is now closed. communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003642-052225
communications.communication:
Subject: Response to Training Records Estimate

Dear Mr. Hugoniot,

Thank you for your update regarding Item 7 of my request. At this time, I do not wish to incur the estimated $500 cost for scanning paper training certificates.

To keep this manageable, I am refining my request:
Please provide a summary or index of training records for current sworn officers that includes officer name, training course title, and completion date. If such an index does not exist, please advise whether the Sheriff’s Office can generate a roster or spreadsheet from its records management system instead of scanning individual certificates.

This refinement should significantly reduce the burden and cost of production while still providing the core information sought.

Thank you,

Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003642-052225
communications.communication:
Subject: Follow-up — Status, Timelines, and Documentation for Outstanding Public Records Requests

Dear Mr. Hugoniot,
I appreciate the records you have provided to date. However, many portions of my requests remain outstanding, and I need a clear, written update on the status of every sub-request generated from my original April 28, 2025, public records request.

Background and relevant submissions
•	Original request submitted: April 28, 2025
•	Refined Tier 1 submission (items 1, 3, 4, 10, 12, 13, 16, 17, 19, 20): submitted via MuckRock on June 15, 2025
•	Refined Tier 2 & 3 submission (items 5–9, 11, 14–15, 18, 21–27): submitted via MuckRock on June 16, 2025
•	Item 7 refinement (training records summary/index requested in lieu of full scanning): submitted via MuckRock on September 1, 2025

Requested information (please provide via the MuckRock portal)
For each sub-request or S00 reference generated from my April 28, 2025, request (for example, S003639-052225, S003640-052225, S003641-052225, S003642-052225, S003643-052225, S003644-052225, S003656-052925, S003657-052925, etc.), please identify whether the item is:
•	Fully completed (and if so, list the files produced and the production date),
•	Partially completed (and if so, list what has been produced and what remains), or
•	Not yet acted upon.

For every outstanding or partially completed item, provide:
•	A firm estimated date (not a vague range) when responsive records, summaries, or indexes will be produced.
•	A short explanation for any additional time needed; and
•	Whether third-party notice, employee sign-off, or other review is required to complete the item. If so, state which step(s) remain.

Item 7 — Training records
In your August 25, 2025, response, you stated that the remaining responsive records were paper-only files totaling approximately 5,000 pages, with an estimated $500 cost to scan. On September 1, 2025, I refined my request to avoid such costs. Please confirm:
1.	Whether a training roster, index, or other summary already exists showing officer name, training course title, and completion date. If so, please produce it.
2.	If no such index exists, whether Jefferson County can generate an extract or report from its existing electronic records management or HR/training systems that contain the same fields, rather than scanning individual certificates.
3.	If neither option is available, please provide written confirmation of that fact and explain what training record formats (electronic or paper) the Sheriff’s Office does maintain.

Items previously marked “no responsive records” (e.g., Item 10 – EIS; Item 20 – BWC misuse)
For each such item, please provide written documentation of the search process used to reach that conclusion, including:
•	The specific databases or physical file locations searched.
•	The staff members who conducted the search (name and title) and the dates of the search; and
•	A brief description of the search method.

Costs and charges
Before incurring any fees, provide an itemized estimate of costs for each installment or production that may require scanning, staff time, or other charges, and identify which items you believe will incur fees. I do not authorize any charges without explicit, prior written cost estimates.

Communications and processing
I will continue to accept communications and records only through the MuckRock portal. Please do not request or require that I reply from a personal or work email address. Submission through MuckRock satisfies the statutory requirement for a records request.

Deadline
Please provide the information above via the MuckRock portal within 10 business days of receipt of this message. If you cannot meet that deadline, provide a written explanation now and a firm date certain by which you will comply.

Statutory note
Under RCW 42.56.520, agencies must respond to records requests promptly and provide reasonable estimates where full production will take time. Continued unexplained delay or failure to provide a status and timeline may leave me with no option but to pursue an administrative appeal and other remedies available under RCW 42.56.

Thank you for your prompt attention. I expect the requested status grid and the Item 7 update as described above.

Sincerely,

Aurora Quinn

Dear Aurora  Quinn: 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Jefferson County Sheriff's Office. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on September 23, 2025 and given the reference number S003952-092325 for tracking purposes. 
 
Records Requested: (5) Disciplinary Records
Please provide records of any sustained misconduct findings for current and former sworn
officers, including reprimands, suspensions, demotions, and terminations, with documentation of
the nature of misconduct and disciplinary decisions. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant Sheriff department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The Public Records Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed.   Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office 
 If you do not see an acknowledgement/Confirmation email from jeffersoncountywa@mycusthelp.net please check your junk mail. You may need to add jeffersoncountywa@mycusthelp.net to your safe senders list.  Communications with Jefferson County in regards to your public records request will come from this email address.

communications.subject: Aurora Quinn records requests
communications.communication:
Hello Aurora,
Today I sent our response to item 7 (S003657).
I will be responding to the rest of your email item-by-item, through this week and next week.
Sincerely,

Ken Hugoniot

Jefferson County Public Records Administrator

1820 Jefferson St.

Port Townsend, WA 98368

(360) 385-9174

khugoniot@co.jefferson.wa.us

Subject: Follow-up — Status, Timelines, and Documentation for Outstanding Public Records Requests
Dear Mr. Hugoniot, I appreciate the records you have provided to date. However, many portions of my requests remain outstanding, and I need a clear, written update on the status of every sub-request generated from my original April 28, 2025, public records request. Background and relevant submissions
• Original request submitted: April 28, 2025
• Refined Tier 1 submission (items 1, 3, 4, 10, 12, 13, 16, 17, 19, 20): submitted via MuckRock on June 15, 2025
• Refined Tier 2 & 3 submission (items 5–9, 11, 14–15, 18, 21–27): submitted via MuckRock on June 16, 2025
• Item 7 refinement (training records summary/index requested in lieu of full scanning): submitted via MuckRock on September 1, 2025 Requested information (please provide via the MuckRock portal) For each sub-request or S00 reference generated from my April 28, 2025, request (for example, S003639-052225, S003640-052225, S003641-052225, S003642-052225, S003643-052225, S003644-052225, S003656-052925, S003657-052925, etc.), please identify whether the item is:
• Fully completed (and if so, list the files produced and the production date),
• Partially completed (and if so, list what has been produced and what remains), or
• Not yet acted upon. For every outstanding or partially completed item, provide: • A firm estimated date (not a vague range) when responsive records, summaries, or indexes will be produced.
• A short explanation for any additional time needed; and
• Whether third-party notice, employee sign-off, or other review is required to complete the item. If so, state which step(s) remain.
Item 7 — Training records In your August 25, 2025, response, you stated that the remaining responsive records were paper-only files totaling approximately 5,000 pages, with an estimated $500 cost to scan.
On September 1, 2025, I refined my request to avoid such costs. Please confirm: 1. Whether a training roster, index, or other summary already exists showing officer name, training course title, and completion date. If so, please produce it. 2. If no such index exists, whether Jefferson County can generate an extract or report from its existing electronic records management or HR/training systems that contain the same fields, rather than scanning individual certificates. 3. If neither option is available, please provide written confirmation of that fact and explain what training record formats (electronic or paper) the Sheriff’s Office does maintain.
Items previously marked “no responsive records” (e.g., Item 10 – EIS; Item 20 – BWC misuse) For each such item, please provide written documentation of the search process used to reach that conclusion, including:
• The specific databases or physical file locations searched.
• The staff members who conducted the search (name and title) and the dates of the search; and
• A brief description of the search method. Costs and charges Before incurring any fees, provide an itemized estimate of costs for each installment or production that may require scanning, staff time, or other charges, and identify which items you believe will incur fees. I do not authorize any charges without explicit, prior written cost estimates. Communications and processing I will continue to accept communications and records only through the MuckRock portal. Please do not request or require that I reply from a personal or work email address. Submission through MuckRock satisfies the statutory requirement for a records request. Deadline Please provide the information above via the MuckRock portal within 10 business days of receipt of this message. If you cannot meet that deadline, provide a written explanation now and a firm date certain by which you will comply. Statutory note Under RCW 42.56.520, agencies must respond to records requests promptly and provide reasonable estimates where full production will take time.
Continued unexplained delay or failure to provide a status and timeline may leave me with no option but to pursue an administrative appeal and other remedies available under RCW 42.56. Thank you for your prompt attention. I expect the requested status grid and the Item 7 update as described above.
Sincerely, Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003952-092325
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of September 23, 2025, Reference # S003952-092325 Dear Aurora  Quinn, The County received the following public records request from you on September 23, 2025: (5) Disciplinary Records 
Please provide records of any sustained misconduct findings for current and former sworn 
officers, including reprimands, suspensions, demotions, and terminations, with documentation of 
the nature of misconduct and disciplinary decisions. 
We have received recent requests for disciplinary records from a couple of other requesters in addition to you. We have been processing third-party notice to the subject employees simultaneously for all requests. Therefore the required third-party notice letters for the first installment of responsive records for this request were sent a couple of weeks ago. We give at least ten day to subjects of the records to review the records and file petitions in court to enjoin release of the records, should they choose to do so. The ten-day period will be complete early next week and the records will be available for release if no petitions are filed in court. We estimate that public records responsive to this request will be available within four business days from today, on or before 10/1/2025. If public records become available in advance of this estimate we will contact you promptly. Conversely, if the County encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you with a revised estimated date. Thank you for your patience in awaiting this information. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Sheriff's Office 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003814-072825
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 29, 2025, Reference # S003814-072825. Dear Aurora  Quinn, Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 29, 2025: (16) Off-Duty Misconduct Records 
Please provide records from the past 10 years involving off-duty misconduct by current or former sworn personnel, including: 
* Incident reports 
* Internal/external investigation documentation 
* Arrests, citations, or disciplinary findings 
There are actually no responsive records for this request, since there have been no rulings of misconduct or disciplinary actions taken for off-duty misconduct during the past 10 years. However, there was one off-duty incident involving Deputy Stevenson which became part of a broader disciplinary investigation, simply because Stevenson failed to submit a report about the incident. There was no finding of misconduct on the part of Officer Stevenson. However, because of this incident's connection to the investigation which resulted in discipline of Officer Stevenson, we are releasing the records related to the off-duty incident. The full Stevenson investigation report (AR 24-27) was included in records for request S003952 (Disciplinary Records), which were also released to you today. Please log in to the Public Records Center at the following link to retrieve the documents described above: Sheriff Public Records Request - S003814-072825  This completes, closes, and provides Jefferson County's final, definitive response to this request. If you have any difficulty retrieving your documents, please contact me for assistance as soon as possible. Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so at your earliest opportunity. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003642-052225
communications.communication:
Subject: Supplemental Clarification and Amendment to April 2025 Public Records Request

Dear Mr. Hugoniot,

This message supplements and amends my April 28, 2025, public records request and subsequent refinements submitted June 15, June 16, and September 19, 2025. It is not a new request but a continuation of the same record chain for administrative clarity.

Status and Timeline Matrix
For each sub-request or S00 reference generated from my April 28, 2025 request (for example, S003639-052225, S003640-052225, S003641-052225, S003642-052225, S003643-052225, S003644-052225, S003656-052925, S003657-052925, etc.), please identify whether the item is:
• Fully completed (and, if so, list the files produced and the production date),
• Partially completed (and, if so, list what has been produced and what remains), or
• Not yet acted upon.

For every outstanding or partially completed item, provide:
• A firm estimated date (not a vague range) when responsive records, summaries, or indexes will be produced;
• A short explanation for any additional time needed; and
• Whether third-party notice, employee sign-off, or other review is required to complete the item. If so, state which step(s) remain.

Item 7 — Training Records
Your August 25, 2025, response estimated $500 to scan roughly 5,000 pages of paper-training records. On September 1, 2025, I refined my request to avoid that cost. Please confirm:
1.	Whether a training roster, index, or other summary already exists showing officer name, training course title, and completion date. If so, please produce it.
2.	If no such index exists, whether Jefferson County can generate an extract or report from its electronic records management or HR/training systems containing those fields, rather than scanning individual certificates.
3.	If neither option is available, confirm that fact and describe the format(s) of training records currently maintained (electronic or paper).

Item 10 — EIS Records
It appears this request was re-opened after an earlier “no responsive records” response. Please explain why the case was re-opened and identify what additional records or reviews were undertaken.
No Responsive Records Items
For all items previously marked “no responsive records” (e.g., Item 10 – EIS; Item 20 – BWC misuse), provide written documentation of the search process used to reach that conclusion, including:
• The specific databases or physical file locations searched;
• The staff members who conducted the search (name and title) and the dates of the search; and
• A brief description of the search method.

Third-Party Notice Verification
Please confirm whether third-party notices have been sent for each relevant item and provide the date each notice was issued. If new notices have been or will be sent, explain why those were not initiated earlier and provide estimated completion dates for the associated records releases.

Staff Roster Updates (2025 only)
Provide a 2025 roster update listing all new hires, resignations, promotions, and reassignments for both sworn and non-sworn personnel. For each entry, include the employee’s name, position title, effective date of the change, and salary (if applicable). If any records exist that summarize these personnel changes for accreditation or reporting purposes, please include those as well.

Costs and Charges
Before incurring any fees, provide an itemized estimate for each installment that may require scanning, staff time, or other charges, and identify which items you believe will incur fees. I do not authorize any charges without explicit written cost estimates.

Communications and Processing
I will continue to accept communications and records only through the MuckRock portal. Please do not request or require that I reply from a personal or work email address. Submission through MuckRock satisfies the statutory requirement for a records request.

Deadline
Please provide the information above via the MuckRock portal within 10 business days of receipt of this message. If you cannot meet that deadline, provide a written explanation now and a firm date certain by which you will comply.

Statutory Note
Under RCW 42.56.520, agencies must respond to records requests promptly and provide reasonable estimates where full production will take time. Continued unexplained delay or failure to provide a status and timeline may leave me with no option but to pursue an administrative appeal and other remedies available under RCW 42.56.

Again, thank you for your prompt attention. I expect the status matrix, Item 7 and Item 10 clarifications, and 2025 personnel update as outlined above.

Sincerely,
Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S004002-101325
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Hugoniot,

I’m following up regarding my April 28, 2025 public records request and its refinements submitted June 15, June 16, September 1, and October 9, 2025. This message is a continuation of that same record chain, not a new request.

It appears that several of my existing items were re-issued under new tracking numbers beginning October 13. While I appreciate the updates, I want to clarify that splitting one original request into multiple new file numbers does not reset statutory timelines under the Public Records Act. All items remain part of my April 28 request, and the clock for compliance runs from that date.
Please provide through the MuckRock portal:

1. Status and timeline matrix
For each sub-request or S00 reference related to my April 28 request (for example, S003639-052225, S003640-052225, S003641-052225, S003642-052225, S003643-052225, S003644-052225, S003656-052925, S003657-052925, and any new S00 numbers generated on 10/13/25):
• Identify whether the item is fully completed, partially completed, or not yet acted upon.
• For any item still outstanding, give a firm estimated date (not a range) for production, a brief explanation for additional time needed, and whether third-party notice or employee sign-off is still pending.

2. Item 7 – Training Records
Confirm whether a training roster or index exists listing officer name, course title, and completion date. If so, please produce it.
If none exists, confirm whether such data can be exported from an electronic system rather than scanning paper certificates, and provide an estimated production date.

3. Item 10 and other “no responsive records” determinations
Provide written documentation of the search process for each item marked “no responsive records,” including:
• Databases or physical files searched;
• Names and titles of the staff who performed the search, with dates; and
• A short description of the method used.

4. Third-party notice verification
Please confirm which items required notice, when those notices were sent, and whether any remain pending.

5. 2025 personnel update
Provide a current roster reflecting new hires, resignations, promotions, and reassignments for both sworn and non-sworn employees during 2025.
Include each employee’s name, position title, effective date of change, and salary (if applicable). If summary documentation exists (for accreditation or reporting), please include it.

6. Cost estimates
Before incurring any fees, continue to provide itemized estimates for any scanning, staff time, or other costs. No charges are authorized without prior written approval.

7. Communications and processing
All correspondence and productions are to continue exclusively through the MuckRock portal. Submission through MuckRock satisfies RCW 42.56 requirements.

8. Statutory note
Under RCW 42.56.520 and RCW 42.56.100, agencies must respond promptly and use the most efficient method to provide records. Repeated re-numbering or fragmentation of an existing request does not constitute a new filing or extend statutory deadlines. Continued delay or failure to provide clear timelines may leave me no option but to pursue an administrative appeal and other remedies under RCW 42.56.

Please provide the requested status update, documentation, and personnel roster within ten business days of this message or supply a written explanation and firm completion date.

Sincerely,

Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: Re: Supplemental Clarification and Amendment to April 2025 Public Records Request
communications.communication:
Hello Aurora,

We have now created new sub-requests in our public records request system for all 27 of the items, or sub-requests, in your public records request. So far, we have been providing records and communications for each item via its matching sub-request in our system. We will continue to operate that way, since helps both us and you stay more organized and efficient with the large amount of work and communication required by your requests.

Prior to this, we have been attempting to work on your requests one at a time since, even though they are "sub-requests", each one of your items is equivalent in size and difficulty to an average individual records request. Therefore because of the overall size and volume of your request, and because of limited staff time, we can try to work on two or three of your sub-requests simultaneously, but even that is difficult and at times our only option is to follow our normal policy and procedure (per the Jefferson County Public Records Act Compliance Policy<https://www.co.jefferson.wa.us/DocumentCenter/View/15574/2022-02-28-Jefferson-County-Public-Records-Act-Compliance-Policy>, Section 6.25) by treating your sub-requests as if they are separate requests and queueing them to work on one at a time.

Now that all of the sub-requests have been created, we will continue to process and update each one as if it was a separate request. Over the next week to ten days, in response to your entreaties, I will attempt to provide an update for each sub-request, either providing records, updating the status, or providing a time estimate for when we think that records may be available. Please be aware that some of the time estimates may still be a few months in the future, depending on how the sub-requests are queued with respect to one another.

Below are a few more specific responses to issues you brought up in your letter:

Item 7: The complete set of summary training records (in spreadsheet files) was provided to you way back in July. Our system shows that you viewed or obtained those records shortly after we provided them to you. Additionally, on September 23, I sent you the following email communication:

"In July, Jefferson County Sheriff's Office already provided all of the training-related summary reports and information that they possess. Our system shows that you viewed or obtained those records shortly after we provided them to you.
As far as we can tell, those records are precisely what you have described in both of your emails and are fully responsive to your refined request. If that is not the case, please reply and let us know in what way they are deficient. If they do not contain the summary information that you want, then the only other option for providing records would be to scan all the paper training files. However, you have already rejected that option due to the cost."

I'm confused as to why you keep asking for these records when it seems clear that you have them in your possession. If I'm misunderstanding something, please let me know clearly what you feel is missing from the records we have provided for item 7. On the other hand, if the spreadsheet files we have provided are satisfactory, please confirm and we will close out this item/sub-request.

Item 10 — EIS Records: We did re-open this sub-request in order to conduct an additional search for records. We have concluded that search and no responsive records were located. We will provide a more complete explanation later this week, when we officially close out this sub-request.

Third-Party Notice Verification: Because you have requested so many personnel-related files, we cannot process all of them at once. Furthermore, as mentioned above, we have limited staff time and many records requests to fulfill, so no employee can be devoted full-time to all of your requests.  Each file needs to be carefully reviewed and redacted for applicable exemptions, before third-party notice can be sent. Additionally, many of the files are paper-only files and it appears that you will not want to pay the scanning fees in all cases. So far, the reports we have provided, which required third-party notice were reports that had recently been scanned and paid for by other requesters, thus we were able to provide them to you free of charge. However, most of the rest of the personnel-related files will need to be scanned in order to provide them, so the next step in the process, before legal review and third-party notice, will be to provide you with additional cost estimates for the scanning fees.

Staff Roster Updates (2025 only): Since we already closed out your sub-request for a staff roster long ago, we will create a new sub-request to provide an updated staff roster.

Sincerely,


Ken Hugoniot

Jefferson County Public Records Administrator

1820 Jefferson St.

Port Townsend, WA 98368

(360) 385-9174

Khugoniot@co.jefferson.wa.us


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Supplemental Clarification and Amendment to April 2025 Public Records Request
Dear Mr. Hugoniot, this message supplements and amends my April 28, 2025, public records request and subsequent refinements submitted June 15, June 16, and September 19, 2025.
It is not a new request but a continuation of the same record chain for administrative clarity. Status and Timeline Matrix For each sub-request or S00 reference generated from my April 28, 2025 request (for example, S003639-052225, S003640-052225, S003641-052225, S003642-052225, S003643-052225, S003644-052225, S003656-052925, S003657-052925, etc.), please identify whether the item is:
• Fully completed (and, if so, list the files produced and the production date),
• Partially completed (and, if so, list what has been produced and what remains), or
• Not yet acted upon.
For every outstanding or partially completed item, provide:
• A firm estimated date (not a vague range) when responsive records, summaries, or indexes will be produced;
• A short explanation for any additional time needed; and
• Whether third-party notice, employee sign-off, or other review is required to complete the item. If so, state which step(s) remain.
Item 7 — Training Records Your August 25, 2025, response estimated $500 to scan roughly 5,000 pages of paper-training records. On September 1, 2025, I refined my request to avoid that cost. Please confirm:
1. Whether a training roster, index, or other summary already exists showing officer name, training course title, and completion date. If so, please produce it.
2. If no such index exists, whether Jefferson County can generate an extract or report from its electronic records management or HR/training systems containing those fields, rather than scanning individual certificates.
3. If neither option is available, confirm that fact and describe the format(s) of training records currently maintained (electronic or paper).
Item 10 — EIS Records It appears this request was re-opened after an earlier “no responsive records” response. Please explain why the case was re-opened and identify what additional records or reviews were undertaken. No Responsive Records Items For all items previously marked “no responsive records” (e.g., Item 10 – EIS; Item 20 – BWC misuse), provide written documentation of the search process used to reach that conclusion, including:
• The specific databases or physical file locations searched;
• The staff members who conducted the search (name and title) and the dates of the search; and
• A brief description of the search method.
 Third-Party Notice Verification
Please confirm whether third-party notices have been sent for each relevant item and provide the date each notice was issued. If new notices have been or will be sent, explain why those were not initiated earlier and provide estimated completion dates for the associated records releases.
Staff Roster Updates (2025 only)
Provide a 2025 roster update listing all new hires, resignations, promotions, and reassignments for both sworn and non-sworn personnel. For each entry, include the employee’s name, position title, effective date of the change, and salary (if applicable). If any records exist that summarize these personnel changes for accreditation or reporting purposes, please include those as well.
Costs and Charges
Before incurring any fees, provide an itemized estimate for each installment that may require scanning, staff time, or other charges, and identify which items you believe will incur fees. I do not authorize any charges without explicit written cost estimates.
Communications and Processing
I will continue to accept communications and records only through the MuckRock portal. Please do not request or require that I reply from a personal or work email address. Submission through MuckRock satisfies the statutory requirement for a records request.
Deadline
Please provide the information above via the MuckRock portal within 10 business days of receipt of this message. If you cannot meet that deadline, provide a written explanation now and a firm date certain by which you will comply.
Statutory Note
Under RCW 42.56.520, agencies must respond to records requests promptly and provide reasonable estimates where full production will take time.
Continued unexplained delay or failure to provide a status and timeline may leave me with no option but to pursue an administrative appeal and other remedies available under RCW 42.56. Again, thank you for your prompt attention.
I expect the status matrix, Item 7 and Item 10 clarifications, and 2025 personnel update as outlined above. Sincerely, Aurora Quinn

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003657-052925. Dear Aurora  Quinn, Jefferson County Sheriff's Office (JCSO) received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (7) Officer Training Records: Please provide detailed training records for all current sworn officers, including certifications, course names, completion dates, and refresher education. On July 8th and July 18th, prior to sending you a cost estimate of about $500.00 for scanning the entirety of JCSO's paper training records, we provided all of JCSO's responsive summary and tabular records regarding the training of current sworn officers. The information contained within those records is sufficiently detailed to cover the types of information that you enumerated. We have observed that those records were downloaded and also posted on the Muckrock website, so we infer that you also found that they sufficiently and adequately fulfilled your request. Those records are still available in the Jefferson County  
Since all summary and tabular records that you requested have been provided, and since you have declined to receive copies of the paper training records, a ll responsive records have been produced and this request is now closed. This completes, closes, and provides Jefferson County's final, definitive response to this request. If you have any difficulty retrieving your documents, please contact me for assistance as soon as possible. Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so at your earliest opportunity. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S004002-101325
communications.communication:
Subject: Outstanding Items – April 28, 2025, Public Records Request (Non-Compliance with Prior Deadline)

Dear Mr. Hugoniot,

This follows my April 28, 2025, Public Records Request and all refinements through October 9 and October 14, 2025.

The ten-business-day deadline specified in my October 14 communication expired on October 28, 2025. As of today, the County is not in compliance with RCW 42.56.520 and RCW 42.56.100.

The following items remain unresolved or deficient:
1.	Status and Timeline Matrix – No matrix has been produced identifying which of the 27 sub-requests are complete, partial, or outstanding. Provide this immediately for all S-numbers associated with the April 28, 2025, request.
2.	Item 7 – Officer Training Records – The July spreadsheets cover only part of the range. Records for 2015–2016 and 2024–2025 training are missing. Please produce those years now. Do not treat this item as closed until confirmed complete.
3.	Item 10 and Other “No Responsive Records” Determinations – Provide the required written documentation showing:
• Databases and physical files searched;
• Names and titles of staff who conducted each search with dates; and
• A brief description of the search methods used.
This applies to Item 10 (EIS) and Item 20 (BWC misuse).
4.	Third-Party Notice Verification – List each item for which third-party notice has been sent, the date sent, and any items still awaiting notice or response.
5.	2025 Personnel Roster Update – Provide the complete update of new hires, resignations, promotions, and reassignments for both sworn and non-sworn personnel during 2025, including name, position title, effective date of change, and salary (if applicable). Any summary records prepared for accreditation or reporting should be included.
6.	Unreadable PDF Files – The two files posted on October 20 display only the message “open in Acrobat X or later.” They cannot be viewed in a standard reader or browser. Please re-issue those records as standard PDFs or ZIP archives of the original documents.
7.	Costs and Estimates – Before any fees are incurred, provide itemized cost estimates per RCW 42.56.120 and identify which items would incur charges. No charges are authorized without my written approval.

To date, Items #1, #2, and #7 remain partially complete; Item #19 appears complete; the remainder have not been satisfactorily addressed.

Repeated re-numbering or fragmentation of the original request does not reset statutory deadlines. Please treat all 27 sub-requests as continuations of the April 28, 2025, filing.

If these deficiencies are not fully resolved within five business days of this message, I will consider the County to be in violation of the Public Records Act and will pursue formal remedies under RCW 42.56.550 without further notice.

Sincerely,

Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: Sheriff Public Records Request :: S004032-102325
communications.communication:
Dear Aurora  Quinn: 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Jefferson County Sheriff's Office. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on October 09, 2025 and given the reference number S004032-102325 for tracking purposes. 
 
Records Requested: Provide a 2025 roster update listing all new hires, resignations, promotions, and reassignments for both sworn and non-sworn personnel. For each entry, include the employee’s name, position title, effective date of the change, and salary (if applicable). Your request will be forwarded to the relevant Sheriff department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. PLEASE NOTE: The Public Records Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed.   Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office
