communications.subject: Re: 10/23/2025 - Outstanding Items – April 28, 2025, Public Records Request (Non-Compliance with Prior Deadline)
communications.communication:
Hello Aurora,

Please see my responses to items in your email received on 10/23/25, interspersed in blue text below:

Subject: Outstanding Items – April 28, 2025, Public Records Request (Non-Compliance with Prior Deadline)
Dear Mr. Hugoniot, this follows my April 28, 2025, Public Records Request and all refinements through October 9 and October 14, 2025.
The ten-business-day deadline specified in my October 14 communication expired on October 28, 2025. As of today, the County is not in compliance with RCW 42.56.520 and RCW 42.56.100.

The ten-business day deadline you specified in the communication received by the county on Oct. 15 at 1:54 AM, WILL expire on Oct. 29. We received this message from you on Oct. 22 at 10:38 PM (effectively received at start of business on Oct. 23).

The following items remain unresolved or deficient:

  1.  Status and Timeline Matrix – No matrix has been produced identifying which of the 27 sub-requests are complete, partial, or outstanding. Provide this immediately for all S-numbers associated with the April 28, 2025, request.

By October 29, (once all sub-requests have been updated) we will be provide a tabular report of all your sub-requests. The report will include our internal request reference numbers (S00- number) and the current status of the sub-request. In the future, upon request, we can also provide similar updated reports of all your sub-requests.


  1.  Item 7 – Officer Training Records – The July spreadsheets cover only part of the range. Records for 2015–2016 and 2024–2025 training are missing. Please produce those years now. Do not treat this item as closed until confirmed complete.

Summary records for 2024 and 2025 are not missing. They are included in the file, 7.Officer_Training.LE.2022toCurrent.csv.
The Sheriff’s Office has completed a search for item #7 and has confirmed that they were unable to locate any summary or tabular records that would have logged trainings completed in 2015. Therefore item #7 is completed and closed.


  1.  Item 10 and Other “No Responsive Records” Determinations – Provide the required written documentation showing: • Databases and physical files searched; • Names and titles of staff who conducted each search with dates; and • A brief description of the search methods used. This applies to Item 10 (EIS) and Item 20 (BWC misuse).

So far, only item #16 has been closed with a status of “no responsive records”. However, in order to be as helpful as we can be, we did provide some records in response to item #16, even though they are not technically responsive. Those records pertain to the only off-duty incident that was logged during the date range of your request. That incident was included in a larger disciplinary investigation, because the officer failed to submit a required report about the incident to the Sheriff’s Office. However, there was no finding of misconduct related to the incident itself. That entire disciplinary investigation case has been provided to you in an installment for item #5 (see case file AR 24-27 Stevenson).

We have provided all responsive records for item #20 and it is completed and closed.

Item #10 remains open because we have concluded that all records related to PIPs, which are also responsive to item #13, would also be responsive to item #10 because PIPs would constitute a form of an early intervention system. If this was not the intention of your request in item #10, please provide appropriate clarification for item #10.


  1.  Third-Party Notice Verification – List each item for which third-party notice has been sent, the date sent, and any items still awaiting notice or response.

We will provide this information where possible. In some cases, the necessity of sending 3rd party notice will depend on whether or not you choose to pay for scanning fees for paper documents (once we have provided estimates).


  1.  2025 Personnel Roster Update – Provide the complete update of new hires, resignations, promotions, and reassignments for both sworn and non-sworn personnel during 2025, including name, position title, effective date of change, and salary (if applicable). Any summary records prepared for accreditation or reporting should be included.

We have logged this as a new records request and will provide responsive records as soon as possible.


  1.  Unreadable PDF Files – The two files posted on October 20 display only the message “open in Acrobat X or later.” They cannot be viewed in a standard reader or browser. Please re-issue those records as standard PDFs or ZIP archives of the original documents.


We have provided standard PDF copies of these records, as you requested.



  1.  Costs and Estimates – Before any fees are incurred, provide itemized cost estimates per RCW 42.56.120 and identify which items would incur charges. No charges are authorized without my written approval.

To date, Items #1, #2, and #7 remain partially complete; Item #19 appears complete; the remainder have not been satisfactorily addressed.

Items #1, #3, #4, #7, #16, #19, and #20 are complete and closed.

Repeated re-numbering or fragmentation of the original request does not reset statutory deadlines.

I have attempted to explain our processing method in a separate email that was sent simultaneously with this email.

Please treat all 27 sub-requests as continuations of the April 28, 2025, filing. If these deficiencies are not fully resolved within five business days of this message, I will consider the County to be in violation of the Public Records Act and will pursue formal remedies under RCW 42.56.550 without further notice.
Sincerely, Aurora Quinn Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2114389/

Sincerely,

Ken Hugoniot
Jefferson County Public Records Administrator
1820 Jefferson St.
Port Townsend, WA 98368
(360) 385-9174
khugoniot@co.jefferson.wa.us

communications.subject: Re: Supplemental Clarification and Amendment to April 2025 Public Records Request
communications.communication:
Hello Aurora,

This email is in reply to your email of October 15th. It addresses the following part of your email: "It appears that several of my existing items were re-issued under new tracking numbers beginning October 13. While I appreciate the updates, I want to clarify that splitting one original request into multiple new file numbers does not reset statutory timelines under the Public Records Act."

On October 13, I finished creating sub-requests in our management system for all of the items in your public records request. One of the sub-requests contains six of the items together. Those six items are grouped together because the majority of the responsive records for each of them are contained together in paper personnel files, so it is more administratively efficient to handle them together. The rest of the 27 items in your request have each been assigned to one corresponding sub-request.

The purpose of the sub-requests is simply for administrative efficiency. Organizing and responding to your records request via the expedient of sub-requests in our management system does not imply any attempt by Jefferson County to avoid or alter statutory timelines. To make sure there is no misunderstanding about this, I have changed the create date for each sub-request to April 30, 2025, the official date of receipt of your request.

We have chosen to use this sub-request procedure because it is the best way to leverage the features of our public records request management system for maximal administrative efficiency and clarity. The efficiency and clarity benefits both you and Jefferson County by providing an efficient clerical structure for our response which allows us to spend more time searching for and providing records to you. At the same time it allows us to spend less time trying to keep track of our progress on each item in your request via some other redundant, and no doubt less-efficient, clerical tool or procedure.

In order to demonstrate what I'm talking about, I've included a screenshot, below, of part of a page from your customer account in our public records management system (i.e. Public Records Center). You will see pages like this when you go to View My Requests in your Public Records Center account. As you can see, one of the sub-requests in the image is labeled "Completed" and the other two are labeled "In Progress". Each one also shows a current "Status" underneath the text of the request. In order to access more details about the current status of any sub-request, all you need to do is click on the "Details" button for any sub-request and then, on the page for that specific sub-request, click on "View Messages" to see the latest communication from Jefferson County regarding that sub-request. If we have provided records in response to any particular sub-request, those records can be viewed and downloaded by clicking on the "View File(s)" button.



[cid:image001.png@01DC3DBD.A70024B0]

You have also asked for an updated matrix or table of all your sub-requests. To fulfill that request, by October 29, we will be providing a tabular report of all your sub-requests. The report will include our internal request reference numbers (S00- number) and the current status of the sub-request. In the future, upon request, we can also provide similar updated reports of all your sub-requests.
Ken Hugoniot
Jefferson County Public Records Administrator
1820 Jefferson St.
Port Townsend, WA 98368
(360) 385-9174

khugoniot@co.jefferson.wa.us
Jefferson County Public Records Administrator
1820 Jefferson St.
Port Townsend, WA 98368
(360) 385-9174

khugoniot@co.jefferson.wa.us<mailto:khugoniot@co.jefferson.wa.us>


Message sent to PRR S003598

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003834-080525
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003834-080525 (12) – Grievance Records 
Please provide formal grievance records filed by or against current or former sworn personnel in the past 10 years, including: 
* Date of grievance 
* Nature of grievance 
* Names or job titles of involved parties 
* Investigation summaries 
* Final outcomes 
Dear Aurora  Quinn, The following is a request for clarification for this item: A "formal grievance" could be interpreted as either a union-related grievance, or as a formal complaint brought by an employee against an individual employee or group of employees. For the purposes of this request, the county will locate and provide records corresponding to both interpretations, unless we receive further clarification from you. As a first installment, we have searched for union-related grievance records. The Human Resources Department has located five responsive union-related grievance case files. the date and employee(s) who brought each grievance are listed below: 02/12/16, Kelli Greenspane 
11/22/16, Kelli Greenspane 
02/02/17, Darrin Dotson 
04/18/17, Teamsters Comm. Staff/Patrol Employees 
07/25/18, JCSO Command Staff These files are in paper format and together they comprise around 1,000 pages or more of documents. Electronic scans of the documents would cost a total of somewhere between $100 to $150. A portion of each grievance file consists of printed emails. It was the standard practice of the previous HR director to print out all emails that were relevant to a case in order to create a comprehensive paper file. One alternative to reduce the amount and cost of scanning (though it would increase production time) would be to sort each file, eliminate all of the email printouts, and provide only scans of the essential formal documents from each file, including the initial complaint, any final report, and all formal correspondence and completed forms. An additional alternative which might eliminate almost all scanning costs, would be to search various locations to individually locate electronic copies of each essential formal document. Most such documents could probably be found as email attachments in the county email archive. This would be least expensive alternative, but also the most time-consuming, with the longest anticipated production time. Please advise which alternative you would prefer and we will proceed accordingly. Sincerely, 
Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S004002-101325
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S004002-101325 (11) Performance Evaluations 
Please provide performance evaluations for current and former sworn officers, including comments, ratings, recommendations. 

(15) Awards and Commendations 
Please provide records of awards or honors given to sworn officers, including dates, types, reasons. 

(21) Records of Transfers and Reassignments 
Please provide records of transfers or reassignments of sworn officers, including reasons and effective dates. 

(23) Promotions and Demotions Records 
Please provide application materials, evaluations, decision memos related to promotions/demotions, dates, justifications. 

(25) Secondary Employment Approvals 
Please provide records showing agency approval for secondary employment by sworn officers. 

(27) Salary Records 
Please provide current and historical salary data for sworn officers, including base pay, overtime, bonuses. 
Dear Aurora  Quinn, We have replaced this sub-request ( reference #S004002) with six separate sub-requests, one for each item listed above. We had put these six items together into this one sub-request because many of the responsive records for each item would be included within employee personnel files. Thus, we thought that it might prove more efficient to create a cost estimate for the scanning fee of all the paper records for these six items, combined. However, now that we've delved into the details of producing records for each item, we've concluded that it would be more helpful and more efficient to create one sub-request for each of these items. Doing that should allow for more clarity in both communication and the process of producing documents. It also conforms to the same clerical procedure that we have used for all of the other items in your records request. We have therefore hereby closed out request reference #S004002, since it is now an unnecessary duplication of the new sub-requests. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Sheriff's Office 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S004042-102825
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S004042-102825 (11) – Performance Evaluations 
Please provide performance evaluations for current and former sworn officers, including comments, ratings, recommendations. 
Dear Aurora  Quinn, We have electronic copies of performance evaluations from the personnel files of eight current or former officers. These were scanned for previous requests therefore they are not necessarily up to date with all recent evaluation records. However, these can be provided free of charge, since the scanning fee does not apply to them. Before releasing the records, we will need to review them for potential exemptions from disclosure and provide third party notice to the eight officers who are the subjects of the records. This will be done along with items #15, and #27. All three will be noticed together to each officer in the same third party letter. Pursuant to this, we estimate that these records will be ready for release within 15 business days, on or before 11/20/25. According to the records search we have conducted so far, the remainder of the performance evaluation records are stored in paper format only. There is quite a large volume of such records, since they occupy a significant portion of each officer's personnel file. We estimate that the total cost to scan all of the responsive paper records would be approximately $300 to $400. If that cost is acceptable to you, then we can begin to process and produce the scanned copies after we have provided the electronic copies mentioned above. Alternatively, if you would prefer for us to start working on the scanned copies before we provide the electronic, already-scanned, records, please let me know as soon as possible. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Sheriff's Office 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S004047-102825 (27) Salary Records 
Please provide current and historical salary data for sworn officers, including base pay, overtime, bonuses. 
Dear Aurora  Quinn, We have electronic copies of payroll status change forms and other salary-related records from the personnel files of eight current or former officers. These were scanned for previous requests therefore they are not necessarily up-to-date with all recent salary-related records. However, these can be provided free of charge, since the scanning fee does not apply to them. Before releasing the records, we will need to provide third party notice to the eight officers who are the subjects of the records. This will be done along with items #11, and #15. All three will be noticed together to each officer in the same third party letter. Pursuant to this, we estimate that these records will be ready for release within 15 business days, on or before 11/20/25. 

The remainder of the payroll status change forms and other salary-related records in personnel files are stored in paper format only. We estimate that the total cost to scan all of these responsive paper records would be approximately $100 to $150. 

If that cost is acceptable to you, then we can begin to process and produce the scanned copies after we have provided the electronic copies mentioned above. Alternatively, if you would prefer for us to start working on the scanned copies before we provide the electronic, already-scanned, records, please let me know as soon as possible. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator

Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S004047-102825
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S004047-102825 (27) Salary Records 
Please provide current and historical salary data for sworn officers, including base pay, overtime, bonuses. 
Dear Aurora  Quinn, For this records request, there is also the potential of producing salary data from payroll records. These could potentially be provided in lieu of the records from officers' personnel files which I described in a separate email. However, producing all of the data that you requested for the ten-year period of your request will not necessarily be straightforward. I've already been informed by payroll that there are no already-existing reports that could cleanly produce all the information you requested, and producing a custom report for that purpose would probably take a very long time. To further complicate things, because of the long date range, data would also have to come out of two completely different databases, necessitating the production of two sets of custom reports. We could, however, explore with you the possibility of creating some kind of simple, limited reports that could be produced more easily and would contain some of the data that is most important to you. In light of the foregoing information, please let us know what you think you want to do in regard to production of payroll data from the county payroll databases. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Sheriff's Office 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003656-052925 (02) Separated Personnel Records: Officer names, badge numbers, ranks/positions, dates of separation, reasons for separation, and any disciplinary or investigation findings related to separated employees. 
Dear Aurora  Quinn, Please provide the following clarification to your public records request: Back in July, we provided a simple spreadsheet report that contained a summary of most of the items you requested in this sub-request. However, it did not include any additional records related to the reasons for each separation, such as resignation letters, or documentation related to retirements and transfers. It also did not include any additional documentation relating to disciplinary or investigation findings. 
 
  *  All additional records related to resignations and retirements will be provided to you via #14. 
  *  All additional records related to transfers and reassignments will be provided to you via #21. All records related to disciplinary investigations (including  will be provided to you via item #5. The only two entries from the separated employees table that could have additional documentation records that may not be provided via #5, #14, or #21 would be the two employees who were terminated while on probation. 
 Given the above information, please clarify whether or not you would like us to provide additional records for this sub-request (item #2) and please specify what those records would be. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003995-101325
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003995-101325 (08) Use of Force Reports 
Please provide reports documenting use of force by sworn officers from January 1, 2015, to present, including type of force, dates, officer names/badge numbers, circumstances. 
Dear Aurora  Quinn, We have provided the records related to Use of Force reports that are available in electronic files. The majority of our Use of Force reports and appended records are filed in paper-format only. All together they comprise a total of around 3,500 pages of documents, since there are a large number of internal affairs UOF files for the 2015-2025 period. Electronic scans (at 10 cents per page) of all these documents would cost a total of around $350. Please let us know as soon as possible whether you are willing to pay these fees. We will continue to process and provide records as soon as we hear from you. Also, if you do not want to pay the scanning fees, please let us know if there is any sub-set of paper records, or other type of electronic records that you think would provide an acceptable alternative to your original request for UOF reports. For instance, we could provide only reports, but not include any of the supplemental documents. Please advise. Sincerely, Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Sheriff's Office 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003995-101325
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
On May 14, 2018, the Jefferson County Board of Commissioners approved Ordinance No. 02 0514 18, authorizing charges for copies of public records, including electronically-transmitted copies. Per Section 3.4 of the Ordinance, these charges are in effect immediately and apply to all pending Public Records Act  requests (this includes new installments of public records provided for pending requests). A copy of the approved Public Records Requests Costs Schedule is available at . For any individual public records request, all costs listed on the schedule are cumulative. However, if total cumulative costs do not exceed $2.00, those charges will be waived. this link  The charges for the records will be $345.00. Once payment is received, the records will be released to you. If payment for the scanned records is not paid within 30 days, we will consider your request closed as abandoned. Charges can be paid by check, cash, or online by credit/debit card. 
Payment by credit or debit card is available online only via the Jefferson County Public Records Center web portal. If payment is expected for a public records request, an invoice will be available when you log in and go to the My Request Center area. The invoice will contain a link to the online payment module. There is a 3% surcharge for all credit/debit card payments, with no minimum flat fee (for example, the surcharge will be $0.09 for a $3.00 payment, $0.30 for a $10.00 payment, $0.60 for a $20.00 payment and so on). 
Payment by check or cash can be made in person at the Board of County Commissioners/County Administrator’s Office (located in the Courthouse basement) or at the County department where records are obtained. If paying by mail, please make your check payable to “Jefferson County” and mail it to Public Records Administrator, Jefferson County, PO Box 1220, Port Townsend, WA 98368. All public records may be inspected on-site free of charge. Records that exist in paper only, generally can be inspected at the record-holding department during normal business hours. A kiosk is available at the Jefferson County Courthouse for inspection of electronic records. If you wish to inspect electronic records, p lease notify the Public Records Administrator by email at publicrecords@co.jefferson.wa.us or by phone at (360) 385-9174. Once you have notified us of this preference, responsive records can be prepared for inspection promptly, as they become available.

Please provide investigative files and administrative reviews of officer-involved shootings for current/former sworn officers. 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office has located records responsive to your request, which are being released to you electronically. Please log in to the Public Records Center at the following link to retrieve your responsive documents: Sheriff Public Records Request - S003996-101325  Some information contained in the responsive records is exempt from production and has been redacted per RCW 42.56 and/or other statutes. Each redaction bears an alphanumeric exemption code that is indexed to the table provided in the file, Current_JeffCo_PRR_Exemption_List. The table provides statutory citations and brief explanatory descriptions of exemptions as required by RCW 42.56.210(3). All responsive records have been produced and the request is now closed. This completes, closes, and provides Jefferson County's final, definitive response to this request. If you have any questions or problems retrieving your documents, please contact the Public Records Administrator at 360-385-9174 or publicrecords@co.jefferson.wa.us. (mailto:publicrecords@co.jefferson.wa.us) Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so at your earliest opportunity. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. 
Sincerely, 
Justin Jackson Sheriff's Office 
Jefferson County

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S004042-102825
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025., Reference # S004042-102825. 
Dear Aurora Quinn, 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: 
(11) – Performance Evaluations 
Please provide performance evaluations for current and former sworn officers, including comments, ratings, recommendations. 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office has located the following records that are responsive to your request: [precise description of all responsive records]. Jefferson County Sheriff's Office has reviewed your request and has determined that the responsive records described above are exempt from disclosure and are therefore being withheld from production for the following reason(s): 
Employee Performance Evaluations are exempt under the PRA except when they discuss specific disciplinary actions. Performance Evaluations that Do Not Discuss Specific Incidents of Misconduct - RCW 42.56.230(3) Dawson v. Daly 120 Wn.2d 782, 845 P.2d 995 (1993)) Explanation: Disclosure of performance evaluations, that do not discuss specific instances of misconduct, is presumed to be highly offensive and no legitimate interest to the public. 
This completes, closes, and provides Jefferson County's final, definitive response to this request. Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so at your earliest opportunity. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. Sincerely, 
Justin Jackson Sheriff's Office 
Jefferson County

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S004001-101325
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S004001-101325 
Dear Aurora Quinn, The Jefferson County Sheriff's Office has an online system for managing the policy updates. There aren't any "memos" that get released, rather an update gets sent to the officers and they have to click into the online policy manual and re-read the section that's been updated. The manual updates in entirety. We have provided each update point for the past 10 years and provided the manual to you. This completes the request. 
The Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: 
(26) Internal Memos Regarding Conduct Policy Changes 
Please provide memos or correspondence about changes to conduct policies or disciplinary procedures. 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office has located records responsive to your request, which are being released to you electronically. Please log in to the Public Records Center at the following link to retrieve your responsive documents: Sheriff Public Records Request - S004001-101325 (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXSx8nJOfUimrtHxfUdR6IHYm5qWqPap4aa1UcfmcubyzH5Q-2F1QsqianQujIUizxMho1wLEJQPMr1msU5gzfdfAA-3Dy4Oa_r1a-2FgsMLMcOiirJbBPZhgGw9TvBZnRMU4RKAkMaTg5b2Nw08AH4qLypnQZ-2Ftp16pAeiW5dY-2FTCsjmPreFqcpS0QU4raresxqb1sS3k038vngtkLNARZFrRdeVOUQRIuaWqZcf0N99i1c-2BI2qNRaCNN55Z69YMyrkzltRwPmPHZuhRWRaoQ-2Bfkjn6RsVbLIMkOmufJvQKqzHefqNnmKKkO4US5fgPv7MGktLMBm7ohaqVBip7zWMJc-2B-2BH3Mmo8biboO6xgVe4FMCw1mWtimVOXlFsxcfnFfWC73BeGfWX8g-2Br-2FCNxbs-2Bp5XKd8NW8A7J6-2F3VWTYfGJbA89vbacAQLPYHqxGl8Phg99saFWaQIDWhOf2p-2BF-2FE6f4lPRnNjA0KPyZ8RqehUKqmCowbu430dBw-3D-3D) All responsive records have been produced and the request is now closed. This completes, closes, and provides Jefferson County's final, definitive response to this request. If you have any questions or problems retrieving your documents, please contact the Public Records Administrator at 360-385-9174 or publicrecords@co.jefferson.wa.us. (mailto:publicrecords@co.jefferson.wa.us) Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so at your earliest opportunity. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. Sincerely,

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S003995-101325
communications.communication:
Subject: April 28, 2025, Public Records Request – Status and Preservation of Rights

Dear Mr. Hugoniot and Mr. Jackson,
I am writing in response to the recent closure notices and status changes issued in January after an extended period of inactivity on this request. To be clear, my April 30, 2025, public records request is not complete, and I do not agree with any characterization that it has been fully satisfied, closed, or abandoned. Over the past several weeks, records have been released in a fragmented and inconsistent manner, including:
•	duplicate uploads
•	files lacking dates or identifying information
•	complex spreadsheets requiring verification
•	multiple items previously identified as pending or estimated for future production
Because of this, I am currently engaged in a good-faith review and inventory of the materials produced to determine:
1.	what has been produced
2.	what remains outstanding
3.	whether prior estimates and representations have been met
Given the volume and condition of the records, this review cannot reasonably be completed on an accelerated timeline created by sudden closure notices issued after months of silence.
Based on the foregoing:
•	I dispute any assertion that this request is complete.
•	I do not waive any rights under RCW 42.56.
•	I do not consent to abandonment or closure while review is ongoing.
•	No consolidated fee has been assessed, approved, or paid for remaining items.
If the Sheriff’s Office contends that specific sub-items are complete, please identify with specificity:
•	which item number(s)
•	what exact records satisfy them
•	which items, if any, the agency believes remain pending or unresolved
If additional records are still anticipated, please confirm that production will continue and that this request will remain open while review proceeds. This message is intended to preserve the record, clarify status, and prevent procedural prejudice while I complete my review. I appreciate your written confirmation that the request will remain open and that no adverse action will be taken during this process.

Sincerely,
Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S004047-102825
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S004047-102825. Dear Aurora Quinn, Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (27) Salary Records 
Please provide current and historical salary data for sworn officers, including base pay, overtime, bonuses. Our records indicate that you have not paid the amount billed for this request or installment within thirty (30) days of being invoiced for records that were prepared for you. Therefore, pursuant to RCW 42.56.120(4) and WAC 44-14-040(10), we have closed this request and consider it to be abandoned. communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S004044-102825. Dear Aurora Quinn, Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (21) Records of Transfers and Reassignments 
Please provide records of transfers or reassignments of sworn officers, including reasons and effective dates. communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S004046-102825
communications.communication:
Subject: Request for Invoice Documentation – S004046-102825
Dear Public Records Administrator,
Your February 13, 2026, notice states that Request S004046-102825 was closed due to nonpayment of an invoice issued for prepared records pursuant to RCW 42.56.120(4).
Please provide:
•	A copy of the invoice is issued for this request.
•	The date of the invoice was transmitted.
•	The amount billed.
•	The payment date was due.
•	Documentation showing that responsive records had been prepared and pending payment were available.
I do not see any invoice reflected in the portal for this sub-request. If no invoice was transmitted, please confirm that the closure was entered in error and reinstate the request.
Please respond within five business days.

Sincerely,
Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S004046-102825
communications.communication:
FORMAL SECTION 9.0 INVOCATION – REQUEST FOR ADMINISTRATIVE REVIEW

Jefferson County Sheriff’s Office Public Records Requests
Original Request Date: April 28, 2025

To:
Public Records Administrator
Jefferson County, Washington

Re: Section 9.0 Administrative Review –
S003995-101325
S004043-102825
S004047-102825
S003952-092325
S004044-102825
S004000-101325
S004045-102825
S004046-102825

Dear Public Records Administrator,
Pursuant to Section 9.0 of the Jefferson County Public Records Act Compliance Policy, I hereby formally invoke the County’s internal administrative review process regarding the above-referenced sub-requests, all stemming from my April 28, 2025, Washington Public Records Act request to the Jefferson County Sheriff’s Office. Please route this invocation to the Prosecuting Attorney’s Office pursuant to Section 9.0 of the Jefferson County Public Records Act Compliance Policy.

This request concerns procedural defects relating to cost authorization, invoice transmission, abandonment determinations, delivery method restrictions, and closure actions taken between February 9 and February 13, 2026.

This invocation is made deliberately under Section 9.0 to ensure full compliance with the County’s internal review procedure.

I. Cost Estimate Authorization Defect
From the outset of the April 30, 2025, request and throughout subsequent communications, I expressly requested an agreed-upon cost estimate prior to production of records.
Despite this condition:
•	Records were produced or claimed to be prepared.
•	Invoices were issued without documented authorization.
•	No written confirmation of cost agreement was obtained.
•	Sub-requests were later closed for alleged “nonpayment.”
Under RCW 42.56.120, agencies may charge fees but may not compel payment absent lawful invoice issuance tied to properly prepared records and proper transmission.
An abandonment determination under RCW 42.56.120(4) presupposes a valid invoice and lawful fee basis.
That predicate is disputed here.

II. Invoice Transmission Defect
Invoices dated January 8 and January 12, 2026, were transmitted via email containing only hyperlink references (SendGrid tracking links), not attached invoice PDFs.
Subsequently, invoice PDFs were uploaded into the thread on later dates.
The timing difference between:
•	Email transmission date, and
•	Attachment upload date
creates material uncertainty regarding when the invoice became accessible and reviewable in complete form.
If the County is calculating a 30-day abandonment period under RCW 42.56.120(4), it must establish:
1.	The date of lawful transmission,
2.	The method of transmission,
3.	That the invoice was accessible in complete form at that time,
4.	Those responsive records had in fact been prepared and were pending payment.
These elements have not been demonstrated.

III. Improper Abandonment of Determinations
Between February 9 and February 13, 2026, the above-listed sub-requests were declared abandoned and closed for nonpayment.
However:
•	Cost authorization was disputed.
•	Delivery issues have been formally raised.
•	A preservation-of-rights notice had been posted.
•	Invoice accessibility and timing were not clear.
Abandonment under RCW 42.56.120(4) requires a valid, properly transmitted invoice tied to prepared records.
If any of those elements are defective, abandonment cannot stand.

IV. Portal-Only Delivery Restriction
Repeated responses directed me to utilize the County’s portal as the exclusive method of communication and payment.
When attempting direct written correspondence, automated “Unrecognized Email” responses were issued.
The Washington Public Records Act does not authorize agencies to impose portal-only access requirements where the requester has designated a delivery method and has objected to portal restriction.
This practice raises additional compliance concerns.

V. Closure While Disputes Were Active
Formal disputes and cure notices were submitted and posted into affected request threads prior to final closure determinations.
Closing requests while documented disputes regarding billing and delivery were pending undermines the integrity of the review process and raises procedural due process concerns under the PRA.

REQUESTED RELIEF
Pursuant to Section 9.0 review, I request the following:
1.	Immediate suspension of abandonment status for all listed sub-requests pending completion of this administrative review.
2.	Reinstatement of the above sub-requests pending the outcome of this review.
3.	A written explanation identifying:
o	The exact transmission method used for each invoice,
o	The transmission date used to calculate the 30-day payment period,
o	Confirmation that responsive records had been prepared prior to invoicing,
o	The legal basis for imposing portal-only delivery requirements.
4.	A written determination addressing whether abandonment determinations were procedurally proper under RCW 42.56.120(4) and WAC 44-14-040(10).
This invocation is made in good faith and for the purpose of ensuring compliance with both the Washington Public Records Act and Jefferson County’s Public Records Act Compliance Policy.
If these matters are not resolved through Section 9.0 review, I reserve all rights under RCW 42.56.550, including the right to seek judicial review within the statutory one-year period.
Please confirm receipt of this Section 9.0 invocation and provide a timeline for review.

Sincerely,
Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #S004046-102825
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I'm following up on the following Washington Public Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on April 29, 2025. You had previously indicated that it would be completed on Feb. 9, 2026. I wanted to check on the status of my request, and to see if there was a new estimated completion date. communications.communication:
RE: Section 9.0 Administrative Review – Receipt and Status Confirmation – PRR #S004046-102825

Dear Public Records Administrator,

I am writing to confirm the receipt and status of my Section 9.0 administrative review invocation submitted on February 27, 2026, regarding the following sub-requests:

S003995-101325
S004043-102825
S004047-102825
S003952-092325
S004044-102825
S004000-101325
S004045-102825
S004046-102825

As of today, I have received no acknowledgment or response confirming that this invocation was received, routed to the Prosecuting Attorney’s Office, or placed under review.

Please confirm:

1. The date my Section 9.0 invocation was received
2. Whether it has been routed to the Prosecuting Attorney’s Office
3. The current status of the administrative review

For clarity, the above-referenced sub-requests remain under active dispute, and any prior abandonment determinations are stayed pending completion of the Section 9.0 review.

Please respond in writing.

Sincerely,
Aurora Quinn

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003643-052225
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003643-052225 Dear Aurora  Quinn, The County received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (10)  Early Intervention System (EIS) Data 
Please provide: 
Records identifying officers flagged for multiple complaints, use of force incidents, or other concerning behavior. Also the following information: 
1. Whether the office has ever maintained an Early Intervention System or similar mechanism. 
2. Whether any policies, communications, or evaluations exist related to this. 
3. Confirmation if no such system or record has existed in the past 10 years. 
4. A description of the search process used to determine no responsive records exist. 
The final installment of records responsive to this request is available to you now in the Jefferson County Public Records Center. The mechanism that Jefferson County Sheriff's Office (JCSO) uses for early supervisory intervention is a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP), in accordance with JCSO Policy 1033. Hence all of the records that have been provided to you in response to your request #13 - "Supervisor Notes and Counseling Records" (Ref. #S003835) have also been provided to you in response to this request, since the same records are responsive to both requests. communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003994-101325
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003994-101325. 
Dear Aurora  Quinn, 

Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: 
(06) Citizen Complaint Records 
Please provide complaints filed by the public against sworn officers from January 1, 2015, to present, including investigation records, findings, and any disciplinary actions. 

The remaining responsive records are all in paper-only format and will have to be scanned in order to provide you with electronic copies. Jefferson County charges a scanning fee of ten cents per page for electronic copies of paper records. There are a total of 324 pages of paper records that are responsive to this request, hence t he total cost for this request is $32.40 . Records will be released upon receipt of payment. Please log in to the Public Records Center at the following link to view and print an invoice (see below for additional information about payment options): 
Sheriff Public Records Request - S003994-101325 (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXSx8nJOfUimrtHxfUdR6IHYm5qWqPap4aa1UcfmcubyzH5Q-2F1QsqianQujIUizxMho1wLEJQPMr1msU5gzfdfAA-3Do6N9_r1a-2FgsMLMcOiirJbBPZhgGw9TvBZnRMU4RKAkMaTg5b2Nw08AH4qLypnQZ-2Ftp16pAeiW5dY-2FTCsjmPreFqcpSyOL9tvv73VGJ3qSnSHwqbfGR9wJqyijLKLMYZN2ku3jEZKOa1AGDa3nZ1ncJ3H64QpWgOuanAc0V3ZpVmsAE97MALuNaSZQxGv5xumO9sX93np6qy7313mYoZCS40NVYk1GCEc3av3JVe8noG7lrRD-2B-2Bxm0ADsFSoHdeIt-2F4qi3DciAMDzEQFv8teI8VLtoGyhQBQK7RzjZplvM-2FZXW245H3n0QYWlrnf6NwoF1UFneex5YAcgfohCiWzuLiigWtFuf-2BQLA-2FCkEObbq3d3u-2B1Rnfd0xsQsn5b5FV0rpHAVgocKNsB45eR179UqR9hsnlQ-3D-3D) 

Charges can be paid by check, cash, or online by credit/debit card. 

Payment by credit card or debit card is available online only via the Jefferson County Public Records Center web portal. If payment is expected for a public records request, an invoice will be available when you log in and go to the My Request Center area. The invoice will contain a link to the online payment module. There is a $2.00 surcharge for all credit/debit card payments. 

Payment by check or cash can be made in person at the Board of County Commissioners/County Administrator’s Office (located in the Courthouse basement) or at the County department where records are obtained. If paying by mail, please make your check payable to “Jefferson County” and mail it to Public Records Administrator, Jefferson County, PO Box 1220, Port Townsend, WA 98368. 

All public records may be inspected on-site free of charge. Records that exist in paper only, generally can be inspected at the record-holding department during normal business hours. If you wish to inspect electronic records, please notify the Public Records Administrator by email at publicrecords@co.jefferson.wa.us or by phone at (360) 385-9174. Once you have notified us of this preference, responsive records can be prepared for inspection promptly, as they become available. 

If payment has not been received, or if you have not claimed or inspected these records within thirty (30) days from today, we will close this request and consider it to be abandoned. 

Sincerely, 

Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Sheriff's Office 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174

RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003656-052925 Dear Aurora  Quinn, The County received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: (02) Separated Personnel Records: Officer names, badge numbers, ranks/positions, dates of separation, reasons for separation, and any disciplinary or investigation findings related to separated employees. 
Last year we provided you with a spreadsheet that we created in response to this request, containing the information you asked for in this request except for full investigative files of disciplinary cases. Since that time we have created an updated version of that spreadsheet and so we are providing that to you in case it is helpful to you. You made a separate request  (#5 or Ref. #S003952) in which you requested all disciplinary records. Hence records of disciplinary investigations have been provided via request S003952. Please log in at the following link to retrieve your responsive records: Sheriff Public Records Request - S003656-052925 (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXSx8nJOfUimrtHxfUdR6IHYm5qWqPap4aa1UcfmcubyzH5Q-2F1QsqianQujIUizxMho1wLEJQPMr1msU5gzfdfAA-3DyqbD_r1a-2FgsMLMcOiirJbBPZhgGw9TvBZnRMU4RKAkMaTg5b2Nw08AH4qLypnQZ-2Ftp16pAeiW5dY-2FTCsjmPreFqcpSxc6D6FAZyu14MctGUGFwDFpAIgZtt0h0NcIRhN6c8GEa2jwTRn0GTQKbIyfMiFsfwca4AA6zIEduz8CwK1qUcrUm7C6iHXgw2k8PlOMG3gucSqpC0JCeVbhvQyrQoBvfzSqg3zsxek-2Bi3b3S76QuFg18V3rSqnpfzXJfvEmupgRaYEkNbCHU1YV-2F-2FcGM8-2By-2FwG4bMH6yI5Rqt8Tw4ZD9n7aDA4BSUu9ihO-2BgA7liKht4y33WJHMTnx4VYJ26nXD62-2BnhXUuG4GK-2B-2FJFaedx-2FWy9QywEEYuDsehIHCftM70wIrp8P1QY7FrHUplzppVfwg-3D-3D) All responsive records have been produced and the request is now closed. This completes, closes, and provides Jefferson County's final, definitive response to this request. If you have any difficulty retrieving your documents, please contact me for assistance as soon as possible. Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.550, y ou may seek judicial review of the county's response to your request for a period of one year from the date of this message. 
If you have any questions or issues regarding any aspect of the county's response to your request, or the records provided to you, please respond directly to this email or contact the Jefferson County Public Records Administrator within twenty (20) days of the date of this message. If you believe that the county has failed to provide any responsive records, either by withholding or omission, you may request a review of the county's response by the Prosecuting Attorney's Office (PAO), pursuant to Section 9.0 of the Jefferson County Public Records Act Compliance Policy. Please provide fitness-for-duty evaluation records for sworn officers. 
Jefferson County Sheriff's Office has determined that there are no documents responsive to your request. This is the final, definitive response from Jefferson County Sheriff's Office to your request. This response completes and closes out Jefferson County Public Records Request #S003999-101325 Jefferson County will take no further action on this request. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.550, y ou may seek judicial review of the county's response to your request for a period of one year from the date of this message.

communications.subject: [Records Center] Sheriff Public Records Request :: S003834-080525
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of April 30, 2025, Reference # S003834-080525. 
Dear Aurora  Quinn, 

Jefferson County Sheriff's Office received the following public records request from you on April 30, 2025: 
(12) – Grievance Records 
Please provide formal grievance records filed by or against current or former sworn personnel in the past 10 years, including: 
* Date of grievance 
* Nature of grievance 
* Names or job titles of involved parties 
* Investigation summaries 
* Final outcomes 

On 10/28/25 we sent you a message regarding this request. We informed you that the responsive records are all in paper format and need to be scanned in order to provide electronic copies. We also provided an estimate of $100 to $150 for the scanning fee, based on an estimate of 1,000 to 1,500 pages of paper records. We did not receive a reply from you regarding the estimate we provided so I am sending an invoice for $100, which would pay for the majority of the responsive records. Once we receive payment for the invoice then we will scan the records and, once they are scanned, send an invoice for whatever additional amount (over $100) is owed to pay for the total pages scanned. 
Sheriff Public Records Request - S003834-080525 (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXSx8nJOfUimrtHxfUdR6IHYm5qWqPap4aa1UcfmcubyzH5Q-2F1QsqianQujIUizxMho1wLEJQPMr1msU5gzfdfAA-3DP0qd_r1a-2FgsMLMcOiirJbBPZhgGw9TvBZnRMU4RKAkMaTg5b2Nw08AH4qLypnQZ-2Ftp16pAeiW5dY-2FTCsjmPreFqcpS4fvCXOV9s1zCkLR6yMjuTZJ43XPm7-2Fm3Ce3FAJk1-2FpYh9ZyjWzEoQwK9-2Fdz3Mmhcj4q1slXf7ksnZmbLWdlAe9VOnJ7k-2Bib-2FkVU2PqeNG5uv0a7Wzd-2FVAXIJ2yut2frJmi1jW1oDYsnBbG9pOCWr7Ac7pcXcw21WiR7lcgVPALLYKFTeV4QYNDs5m-2FdlTrcLzVP6z4RaH7YfJ2EYnU5poc-2BU4vEwcFTI-2FOet-2FX-2F-2FzU-2B5ybvoeRcf2ymJDfzppgSH94oOM3BEtJIt6ZtEkYM5gkguvwbO-2BmkPSh4-2F1PYXw3ffGhiweGTQakHCAfoC6xUKQ-3D-3D) 

Charges can be paid by check, cash, or online by credit/debit card. 

Payment by credit card or debit card is available online only via the Jefferson County Public Records Center web portal. If payment is expected for a public records request, an invoice will be available when you log in and go to the My Request Center area. The invoice will contain a link to the online payment module. There is a $2.00 surcharge for all credit/debit card payments. 

Payment by check or cash can be made in person at the Board of County Commissioners/County Administrator’s Office (located in the Courthouse basement) or at the County department where records are obtained. If paying by mail, please make your check payable to “Jefferson County” and mail it to Public Records Administrator, Jefferson County, PO Box 1220, Port Townsend, WA 98368. 

All public records may be inspected on-site free of charge. Records that exist in paper only, generally can be inspected at the record-holding department during normal business hours. If you wish to inspect electronic records, please notify the Public Records Administrator by email at publicrecords@co.jefferson.wa.us or by phone at (360) 385-9174. Once you have notified us of this preference, responsive records can be prepared for inspection promptly, as they become available. 

If payment has not been received within thirty (30) days from today, we will close this request and consider it to be abandoned. 

Sincerely, 

Ken Hugoniot 
Public Records Administrator 
Central Services 
Jefferson County 
360-385-9174 

To monitor the progress or update this request please log into the Public Records Center (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXSx8nJOfUimrtHxfUdR6IHYm5qWqPap4aa1UcfmcubyzhAfZbb-2BTTeGBgFfrpJ813ApjwtZgDpX4MB7rIduRYh-2BUFyg2q20SEd8Ke0YYLkS6fZYn_r1a-2FgsMLMcOiirJbBPZhgGw9TvBZnRMU4RKAkMaTg5b2Nw08AH4qLypnQZ-2Ftp16pAeiW5dY-2FTCsjmPreFqcpS4fvCXOV9s1zCkLR6yMjuTZJ43XPm7-2Fm3Ce3FAJk1-2FpYh9ZyjWzEoQwK9-2Fdz3Mmhcj4q1slXf7ksnZmbLWdlAe9VOnJ7k-2Bib-2FkVU2PqeNG5uv0a7Wzd-2FVAXIJ2yut2frJmi1jW1oDYsnBbG9pOCWr7Ac7pcXcw21WiR7lcgVPALLx-2B3DjXkoRLTZiNA1BG3VEBfzHs38KcN53sp09q9P4BSSUXngChtbr0h7iVHq7lSSTzS980mokYEyNSCjeXSbd8ItGfj4tQ-2Fxixig7ietlntN-2B0Ohz-2BeztQdWKXu1v8tMG4wRHJE0BCsH4rc6LrxjqQ-3D-3D)

communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Side Jobs (Bell Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To whom it may concern,
Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Gov. Code § 7920.000 et seq.), I am requesting copies of records related to secondary or off-duty employment as described below.
Please provide all secondary employment request forms, approvals, denials, disclosures, or related records submitted from January 1, 2024 to the present, including but not limited to:
. Secondary employment request or approval forms
. Any attachments or employer disclosures submitted with those forms
. Records reflecting the employer name, type of employment, and date(s) of
approval or denial
. Any logs, databases, or summaries used to track secondary employment
requests during this time
- Policies around secondary employment

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

TACO INTL

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Secondary Employment (Bell Police Department)
communications.communication:
Good evening,

The Bell Police Department has identified responsive records regarding your request for secondary employment request forms, approvals, denials, disclosures, or related records submitted from January 1, 2024, to the present.

The Department is withholding certain responsive records from disclosure. The records are maintained as peace officer personnel records and contain information obtained from or relating to those personnel records, including personal data, employment-history information, and other private information. Accordingly, the records are exempt from disclosure under Penal Code sections 832.7(a) and 832.8, and Government Code section 7927.705.

The records also contain private personnel and personal information, the disclosure of which would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy under Government Code section 7927.700. In addition, based on the facts of this request and the nature of the records, the public interest served by withholding the records clearly outweighs the public interest served by disclosure pursuant to Article 1, Section 1 of the California Constitution and Government Code section 7922.000.

The Department has reviewed the records for reasonably segregable non-exempt information and has determined that the exempt information is inextricably intertwined with the substance of the records. Accordingly, there is no reasonably segregable non-exempt portion available for disclosure.

The Department is not aware of any additional responsive logs, databases, summaries, denials, or other non-exempt responsive records.

This completes the Department’s response to your request.

Thank you.



Esbeyda Pimentel
Police Records Supervisor
Bell Police Department
6326 Pine Avenue
Bell CA 90242
323-923-1564

[cid:image002.png@01DCD9BB.EFE73110]

title: AB 715, Ethnic Studies Curricula (Acalanes Union High School District)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: AB 715, Ethnic Studies Curricula (Acalanes Union High School District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Materials and correspondence sent or received by the district Board of Education or district administrators containing the following terms and/or any variations of these terms: “Assembly Bill (AB) 715” and “Alliance for Constructive Ethnic Studies (ACES)” from September 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. The search should include both interdepartmental and external communications.

2.  Materials and correspondence sent or received by the district Board of Education or district administrators containing the following terms and/or any variations of these terms: “ethnic studies”; “International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance (IHRA)”; “antisemitism,” “Israel,” and “Palestine” from September 1, 2025 to the date this request is processed. The search should include both interdepartmental and external communications. Please exclude mass mailers.

3. Materials and correspondence between the district Board of Education or district administrators and representatives of the Alliance for Constructive Ethnic Studies (ACES), the Foundation Against Intolerance and Racism (FAIR), Combat Antisemitism Movement (CAM), Jewish Community Relations Council (JCRC), Oakland Jewish Alliance, Bay Area Jewish Coalition, or Project Shema, from September 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. The search should include both interdepartmental and external communications. Please exclude mass mailers.

4. Records relating to any change, proposed change, or review of district ethnic studies curricula from September 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. This request should include, but is not limited to, correspondence discussing proposed or actual changes; circulated draft curricula or instructional materials; meeting agendas, minutes, or presentations of the district Board of Education or district administrators in which changes to district ethnic studies curricula were discussed; recommendations or input from legal counsel, consultants, or other agencies regarding changes to district ethnic studies curricula; and public communications or notices announcing or explaining any changes to district ethnic studies curricula during the period of the request.

I am a member of the media, and the documents will be used in the course of reporting. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

If there are fees associated with fulfilling my request, I would be grateful if you could inform me of the total charges in advance. I would prefer the request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.

I look forward to receiving your response within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. Thanks in advance for your work on this request.

Sincerely,

Marianne Dhenin

communications.communication:
March 30, 2026

Via Electronic Mail Only 
Marianne Dhenin
MuckRock.com
204441-76724647@requests.muckrock.com

Re: February 18, 2026 California Public Records Act Request 

Ms. Dhenin:

This letter follows the Acalanes Union High School District’s (“District”) February 27, 2026 response to your February 18, 2026 request for records under the California Public Records Act (“CPRA”; Gov. Code § 7920.000 et seq.).

Attached are non-exempt and non-privileged records responsive to your request. Please note the District has redacted personnel information or similar files, the disclosure of which may constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, and withheld records that would identify individual students in violation of the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (“FERPA”). (See 20 U.S.C. § 1232g; Gov. Code, §§ 7927.700, 7927.705, 7922.000; Cal. Const. art. I § 1.)

To the extent the District inadvertently produces any document(s) which are subject to an exemption under the CPRA or any state or federal laws, such production is not intended to be a waiver of any privilege or any right to withhold such document(s) or documents of a similar nature. (See, e.g., Gov. Code, § 7921.505; see also Ardon v. City of Los Angeles (2016) 62 Cal.4th 1176.)

Please be advised that the District is still in the process of reviewing records that are potentially responsive to your request. This process yields a voluminous amount of email records that must be reviewed before they can be produced. Thus, the District intends to provide responsive records to you on a rolling basis rather than holding records until all responsive records have been gathered and reviewed. Accordingly, the District intends to provide you with copies of additional non-privileged, non-exempt public records responsive to your request on or about April 17, 2026.

The District is aware of its obligation under Government Code section 7922.600 to assist you in making a more focused and effective request that reasonably describes an identifiable record or records. To the extent the District has misinterpreted your request and/or you are able to provide clarification regarding specific records you are seeking, please feel free to contact me at your convenience.

Sincerely,

John Walker
Associate Superintendent, Educational Services
Attachments

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P001220-021826
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of Pasco City Clerk's Office. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 2/18/2026 and given the reference number P001220-021826 for tracking purposes.  NOTE:  The day the request is received does not count as one of the five (5) days. Weekends and holidays observed by the agency are also excluded in the calculation.  If your request has been submitted on the weekend, or on a holiday, the date received is the next business day. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Mission documentation of each UAS (Unmanned Aircraft System, also known as "drone") deployment in 2025, sufficient to determine the date, time, location, and purpose of each deployment. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2216686/ 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the City of Pasco Washington Public Records Center. 
Pasco City Clerk's Office

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Personnel File, Trista Harper
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Please supply the full personnel files of the following individuals:

Trista Harper, current principal of Hancock High School

This includes any resumes, applications, any job recommendations, promotions, professional license credentials and verification records. Please include a work/salary history. Please include any investigative materials - investigative memorandums and interview summary reports, disciplinary actions (suspensions, leaves of absence that stem from disciplinary action), incident reports, EOCO investigations, Office of Civil Rights determinations, non-disciplinary memorandum of understandings, non-union misconduct form, full warning resolutions and any complaints or claims/lawsuits/settlements. Please include any safety plans. Please include any separation agreements and any other disclosure agreements.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019305-032226
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
May 1, 2026 Miriam Bhimani 
RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019305-032226 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: This letter is in reply to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request seeking the following information: 
Trista Harper, current principal of Hancock High School 
This includes any resumes, applications, any job recommendations, promotions, professional license credentials and verification records. Please include a work/salary history. Please include any investigative materials - investigative memorandums and interview summary reports, disciplinary actions (suspensions, leaves of absence that stem from disciplinary action), incident reports, EOCO investigations, Office of Civil Rights determinations, non-disciplinary memorandum of understandings, non-union misconduct form, full warning resolutions and any complaints or claims/lawsuits/settlements. Please include any safety plans. Please include any separation agreements and any other disclosure agreements. RESPONSE : Documents responsive to your FOIA request are available to download via the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. Please visit the following link and enter your username and password to access the records: http://cps.edu/About_CPS/Departments/Law/Pages/FOIARequest.aspx . Under the rules of FOIA, private information and unique identifiers contained within public records are exempt from disclosure and have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7 (1)(b), “Private information, unless disclosure is required by another provision of this Act, a State or federal law or a court order,” and pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c), “Personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subjects of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs the legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. The disclosure of information that bears on the public duties of public employees and officials shall not be considered an invasion of personal privacy. Under the rules of FOIA, identifiable student-level confidential information included within records are exempt from disclosure pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, section 7.5(r) exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at its direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored. 105 ILCS 10/2(d): Release of a minor’s record may only be authorized by the parent or guardian in accordance with the Illinois Student Records Act (105 ILCS 10/1 et seq.). Portions of the information you seek are exempt from disclosure pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a) corresponding with 105 ILCS 5/34-18.5(b) which prohibits the release of background check information. nformation has additionally been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(a): Information specifically prohibited by federal or State law or rules and regulations implementing federal or state law. Specifically, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA). Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a), this section exempts from disclosure information specifically prohibited from disclosure by federal or State law or rules and regulations implementing federal or State law. Specifically, the Abused and Neglected Child Reporting Act, section 325 ILCS 5/1, states that all records concerning reports of child abuse and neglect or records concerning referrals under this Act and all records generated as a result of such reports or referrals, shall be confidential and shall not be disclosed except as specifically authorized by this Act or other applicable law. Redactions were made to conceal individual identities pursuant to Section 7(1)(d)(iv) which allows a public to withhold records/information that would unavoidably reveal the identity of a complainant or witness. Additionally, please note that records of discipline more than four years old are exempt from disclosure pursuant to section 7.5(q) of the FOIA (5 ILCS 140/7.5(q)), which exempts information prohibited from being disclosed by the Personnel Records Review Act [820 ILCS 40/0.01 et seq.]. 820 ILCS 40/8 of the Personnel Records Review Act states that “an employer shall review a personnel record before releasing information to a third party and, except when the release is ordered to a party in a legal action or arbitration, delete disciplinary reports, letters of reprimand, or other records of disciplinary action which are more than 4 years old.” Lastly, be advised that records have been withheld pursuant to section 7(1)(m) of the FOIA, exempts communications between a public body and an attorney representing the public body that would not be subject to discovery in litigation including attorney-client privileged material. To the extent you consider this a denial of your request, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a) you may file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at: Public Access Counselor; Office of the Attorney General; 500 South 2nd Street; Springfield, IL 62706 . You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court; 5 ILCS 140/11.  Thank you for your request. Sincerely, Ana Diaz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools
