title: Records Request

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to, and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFP# 25-220

Title/Description: Bus Stop-Arm Cameras

Issuing Entity: King County Directors' Association

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 

All addenda to the solicitation, and any questions and answers (Q&A) or clarifications issued to vendors. 

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

Attention requester,

The purpose of this email is to acknowledge receipt of your Public Records Act request to King County Directors Association dated 03/31/2026, pursuant to RCW 42.56.520. KCDA's local public records policy can be found here: https://www.kcda.org/file%20library/public%20records%20policy%20and%20procedures/kcda_public_records_policy_and_procedures.pdf

KCDA estimates that all records responsive to your request will be made available to you no later than 05/01/2026. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.520, this time may be needed in order to locate and assemble documents responsive to your request; to notify third persons affected by the request; and to determine whether any of the information requested is exempt from public disclosure.

Kindest regards,

KCDA

I am writing regarding the Public Records Act (PRA) request you submitted to KCDA on 03/31/2026. At this time, all non-exempt public records responsive to your request have been identified and produced via this Google Drive link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a_y9tpV4Q0m67OADEZ3fjwKK6G3PLoNL?usp=sharing We have waived the $2.18 fee for the 174 electronic files.

Kindest regards,

title: Tyler County, WV Opioid Settlements Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Tyler County, WV Opioid Settlements Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Drew Solt 
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 24 2026

P.O. Box 66
Middlebourne, WV
26149

To Whom It May Concern, 

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to County Name opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, Drew Solt
drewsolt@yahoo.com
740-243-6793

title: Open Records Request — Communications Regarding Flock Safety Live Stream Access, March 2026

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request — Communications Regarding Flock Safety Live Stream Access, March 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All written communications — including emails, text messages, direct messages, platform notifications, and any other recorded communications — between any employee, officer, or agent of the City of Dunwoody or the Dunwoody Police Department and any employee or representative of Flock Safety, sent or received between February 23rd, 2026 and March 31, 2026, that reference, discuss, or relate to any of the following subjects:

1. Live stream or live view access to Dunwoody camera networks within the Flock Safety platform
2. The "Can View Live Stream For LPR" organization-level setting within the Flock Safety platform
3. Network sharing permissions, including but not limited to the canViewLiveStream permission, for any Dunwoody camera network
4. Any changes to network sharing settings, discoverability settings, or permission configurations within the Flock Safety platform
5. Preparation for, or discussion of, any presentation to the Dunwoody City Council regarding the Flock Safety platform

This request includes but is not limited to communications sent or received by Lt. Tim Fecht, Chief Carlson, City Manager Eric Linton, Technology Director Ginger LePage, or any other city or department personnel with administrative access to the Flock Safety platform.

Please provide all responsive records in their native electronic format where possible. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a written explanation identifying the specific statutory exemption claimed for each withheld record.

  Description:  To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All written communications — including emails, text messages, direct messages, platform notifications, and any other recorded communications — between any employee, officer, or agent of the City of Dunwoody or the Dunwoody Police Department and any employee or representative of Flock Safety, sent or received between February 23rd, 2026 and March 31, 2026, that reference, discuss, or relate to any of the following subjects: 

1. Live stream or live view access to Dunwoody camera networks within the Flock Safety platform 
2. The "Can View Live Stream For LPR" organization-level setting within the Flock Safety platform 
3. Network sharing permissions, including but not limited to the canViewLiveStream permission, for any Dunwoody camera network 
4. Any changes to network sharing settings, discoverability settings, or permission configurations within the Flock Safety platform

title: DOD involuntary trans records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: DOD involuntary trans records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Reports, policy memos, forms, form responses, emails, text messages, separation documents and other records regarding active-duty and reserve forces service members who have been diagnosed with or “exhibit symptoms consistent with” gender dysphoria and have been identified for involuntary separation. I request records both from the Department of Defense connected to the separations, guiding policy and their requirements under Executive Order 14183, as well as records regarding individual service members' identification and involuntary separation.

I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

I request a complete waiver of all search and duplication fees. If my request for a waiver is denied, I request that I be considered a member of the news media for fee purposes. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request.

Under the FOIA Improvement Act of 2016, agencies must adopt a presumption of disclosure, withholding information “only if . . . disclosure would harm an interest protected by an exemption” or “disclosure is prohibited by law.”

The FOIA Improvement Act of 2016 also amended the FOIA as follows (5 USC 552(a)(8)):

(A) An agency shall—
(i) withhold information under this section only if—
(I) the agency reasonably foresees that disclosure would harm an interest protected by an exemption described in subsection (b); or
(II) disclosure is prohibited by law; and
(ii) (I) consider whether partial disclosure of information is possible whenever the agency determines that a full disclosure of a requested record is not possible; and
(II) take reasonable steps necessary to segregate and release nonexempt information. . .

In the event some portions of the requested records are properly exempt from disclosure, please disclose any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions of the requested records. If it is your position that a document contains non-exempt segments, but that those non-exempt segments are so dispersed throughout the document as to make segregation impossible, please state what portion of the document is non-exempt, and how the material is dispersed throughout the document. Claims of nonsegregability must be made with the same degree of detail as required for claims of exemptions in a Vaughn index. If a request is denied in whole, please state specifically that it is not reasonable to segregate portions of the record for release.

Further, “the withholding agency must supply ‘a relatively detailed justification, specifically identifying the reasons why a particular exemption is relevant and correlating those claims with the particular part of a withheld document to which they apply.’”

In addition, I ask that your agency exercise its discretion to release records which may be technically exempt, but where withholding serves no important public interest.

If a portion of the responsive records become available before the entire request is complete, I respectfully request that the department provide records on a rolling basis.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand
